# CVE-2026-35273 - Oracle PeopleSoft Environment Management Hub Exploitation Kit  # Overview CVE-2026-35273 is a critical, unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in the **Updates Environment Management** component of Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools. The flaw has been actively exploited in the wild by UNC6240 (ShinyHunters) between 2026-05-27 and 2026-06-09, prior to Oracle publishing the public advisory.  # Exploit Complexity This is **not** a trivial attack. Full exploitation requires a multi-stage chain:  1. **SSRF trigger** → internal host reachability assessment 2. **Internal footprinting** → identify deployment topology, middleware, cloud metadata 3. **Webshell deployment** → JSP upload through EMHub write interfaces 4. **Post-exploitation** → recon, lateral movement, persistence, cleanup  The kit automates every stage, with graceful degradation when individual stages fail. #oracle #peoplesoft #0days #exploit #security #hacking #CVE #CVSS @securityaffairs @lorenzofb @TechCrunch

stop bookmarking 30 different pentest repos.  Someone consolidated 150+ red team tools and 19 field-tested tips into one repo, and mapped every single one to the MITRE ATT&CK kill chain. This one walks you through the entire attack lifecycle in order:  → Reconnaissance: 24 tools including spiderfoot, nuclei, reconftw, gowitness → Resource Development: 12 tools including msfvenom, Shellter, Chimera, Freeze → Initial Access: 10 tools including CredMaster, EvilGoPhish, SET, Hydra → Execution: 13 tools including Responder, secretsdump, evil-winrm, Rubeus → Privilege Escalation: 11 tools including LinPEAS, WinPEAS, Certify, Watson → Defense Evasion: 8 tools including Invoke-Obfuscation, ScareCrow, AMSI Fail → Credential Access: 11 tools including Mimikatz, hashcat, nanodump, LaZagne → Lateral Movement: 12 tools including crackmapexec, PsExec, kerbrute, Coercer → Command and Control: 9 tools including Havoc, Covenant, Merlin, NimPlant → Exfiltration + Impact: 9 tools including Dnscat2, Cloakify, PyExfil, usbkill  Most entries ship with install commands, usage examples, and screenshots. Every tip is credited to the operator who originally shared it.  Marked for informational and educational use only.  8.8k stars. 1.2k forks.

sample_texts: Asia-Pacific has become the most underestimated cyber insurance market in the world. A new INTERPOL

sample_tweets.text: Asia-Pacific has become the most underestimated cyber insurance market in the world. A new INTERPOL cyber threat assessment suggests insurers may need to rethink how they underwrite cyber risk across the region.  Unlike market reports, this is law enforcement intelligence compiled from 18 member countries and private-sector partners. That gives the findings particular relevance for cyber insurers.  The numbers are striking:  🔹 135,000+ ransomware attacks recorded in 2025 🔹 6.5 bn cyber threats detected and mitigated across the region in 2024 🔹 Deepfake-related criminal discussions surged 600% in just five months 🔹 DDoS attacks increased 92% year-on-year 🔹 Cybercrime accounts for over 30% of all recorded crime in more than half of surveyed countries 🔹 Scam centres generate nearly $40 bn annually  The threat landscape is changing rapidly.  Ransomware remains a high-severity exposure, with average claims reaching $508,000 according to At-Bay. Social engineering claims in APAC rose 233% year-on-year, while AI-powered fraud is moving from experimentation to operational deployment.  The most revealing examples involve deepfake-enabled fraud.  In Hong Kong, an employee transferred $25 mn after participating in a video call with AI-generated executives. In Singapore, a finance director nearly lost $499,000 in a similar attack.  These incidents challenge traditional policy wording. Many social engineering and business email compromise forms were not designed for real-time synthetic video impersonation.  From my perspective, the core underwriting issue is accumulation risk. A cyber event that originates in a lower-maturity jurisdiction can spread rapidly across regional operations, suppliers, and cloud ecosystems. Cyber risk in Asia-Pacific increasingly behaves as a regional exposure rather than a collection of local policies.  The market opportunity is significant as digitalization accelerates and regulation expands across Singapore, Japan, South Korea, and Southeast Asia. But underwriting profitability will depend on how quickly insurers update assumptions built for a very different threat environment.

sample_tweets.text: Don't let the Lazarus Group dump on my chart again, Houdini.

At the recent G7 summit in Evian, France, world leaders agreed to work together against North Korea’s growing crypto theft and cybercrime activities. The move comes after a series of major attacks linked to North Korean hackers, including the infamous $1.5 billion Bybit hack.  G7 leaders raise concerns that crypto theft is funding North Korea’s nuclear program.  G7 Warns Stolen Crypto Could Be Funding Nuclear Program  In a joint statement released on 18 June, G7 leaders warned that North Korea’s cryptocurrency thefts and cybercrimes now pose a global security threat.  According to the United Nations and several cybersecurity firms, North Korean hacking groups have used stolen crypto funds to help finance the country’s weapons programs.  “We express deep concern about North Korea’s nuclear and ballistic missile programs and reaffirm our commitment to the complete denuclearization of North Korea in accordance with UN Security Council resolutions.”  The group urged member nations to work together to combat the growing wave of attacks while also expressing concern over North Korea’s nuclear and ballistic missile programs.  “We urge North Korea to resolve the abductions issue immediately. We reiterate the need to jointly address North Korea’s cryptocurrency thefts and cybercrimes.”  Billions Continue To Flow To North Korean Hackers  The G7’s warning comes as North Korea-linked crypto thefts continue to rise. The Lazarus Group, a hacking organization widely linked to North Korea, has been blamed for several high-profile attacks on crypto exchanges and DeFi platforms.  According to Chainalysis, hackers connected to the country stole at least $2 billion in 2025, pushing the total amount linked to DPRK-backed groups to around $7.35 billion by 2026.  North Korea is now responsible for a large share of global crypto thefts, accounting for 64% of all stolen crypto in 2025 and 76% of losses recorded in early 2026.  Researchers say hackers are also changing tactics, often posing as recruiters, investors, or IT workers to gain access to crypto companies.  Recent incidents linked to North Korean actors include the $285 million Drift Protocol exploit and the $36 million Humanity Protocol hack.  What G7 Is Planning Next?  Well, the G7 did not announce specific penalties, sanctions, or enforcement measures.  However, the latest statement suggests governments are preparing to treat crypto-related cybercrime much differently than before.  That could mean stronger blockchain surveillance, tighter compliance requirements for exchanges, and closer international cooperation on tracking stolen funds.

⚡️2025 był rekordowy pod względem wartości dzięki jednemu mega-hackowi: - Bybit (~$1,4–1,5B w lutym 2025) — największy hack w historii krypto, przypisywany Lazarus Group (północnokoreańscy hakerzy). To jeden incydent, który zdominował cały rok. W 2026 sytuacja się odwróciła: - Q1 2026: „tylko” ~$169M przy 34 incydentach — relatywnie spokojnie. - kwiecień 2026: Najgorszy miesiąc w historii pod względem liczby ataków (28–30 incydentów, ~$600–630M). Dwa ogromne hacki: Kelp DAO (~$292M) + Drift Protocol (~$285M). Razem to ~75–95% strat kwietnia. - do maja/czerwca 2026: Łącznie już ponad $840M straconych w 2026, przy 50+ incydentach. Czerwona linia na wykresie właśnie dlatego tak ostro rośnie.  Poszperałem i: Nastąpiła fundamentalna zmiana wektora ataków. Około 72% strat w 2026 wynika nie z błędów w smart kontraktach, lecz z kompromitacji kluczy prywatnych, social engineering i słabości operational security. Nawet protokoły z audytami, multisig i timelockami padają, gdy atakujący (głównie grupy powiązane z Koreą Północną — Lazarus Group) przejmują kontrolę nad procesami ludzkimi i infrastrukturalnymi. Państwowi aktorzy odpowiadają za ~76% wartości strat przy minimalnej liczbie ataków — koncentracja ryzyka jest ekstremalna. Implikacje: Wzrost liczby incydentów przy jednoczesnym dojrzewaniu największych protokołów wskazuje na paradoks skali ekosystemu. Większe TVL generuje większą powierzchnię ataku, a nakłady na bezpieczeństwo techniczne nie nadążają za ewolucją taktyk hakerów. #DeFi #CryptoSecurity #Hacks

Crypto firms are spending heavily on security audits, but hackers are still making off with billions of dollars. According to a new report from Oak Security, many of the biggest attacks no longer target flaws in smart contract code. Instead, attackers are exploiting stolen credentials, weak internal controls, and operational mistakes.  Since 2022, cybercriminals, including North Korea’s Lazarus Group, have stolen more than $2.2 billion from crypto platforms. Over the same period, the industry sharply increased the number of code audits. However, many major security breaches have originated from areas that traditional audits are not designed to evaluate, including private key management, governance mechanisms, and internal security controls.  The report points to a growing gap between what audits can protect and how attackers now operate. As a result, security experts say crypto firms need to look beyond code and strengthen the systems and processes that protect customer funds.  Attackers Shift Beyond Smart Contracts  Code audits have become far more sophisticated, helping developers catch vulnerabilities before projects go live and reducing the number of flaws found in smart contracts. But as the technology has improved, hackers have changed their approach.  Attackers are trying to exploit humans and systems inside an organization rather than bugs in coding. Such types of attacks include phishing attacks, stealing private keys, exploitation of system updates, and internal threats. Many large-scale thefts in recent times have been due to such attacks, not flaws in the coding of applications.  Researchers said audits are still working as intended, identifying security issues before deployment. The problem is that audits can only assess code. They cannot prevent an employee from handing over credentials, approving a fraudulent transaction, or falling victim to a phishing attack. As a result, strong code is no longer enough to protect a crypto platform on its own.  Related: Binance at Risk of Losing EU Access as Greece Rejects MiCA Licence  False Confidence Creates New Risks  Crypto projects often point to security audits as evidence that their platforms are safe, highlighting completed reviews and reports from auditing firms. For many users, those audits can create the impression that a project is protected from major security failures.  Researchers say that assumption can be misleading. An audit only evaluates a project’s code at a specific point in time. New risks can emerge as platforms update their infrastructure, change governance structures, or expand operations.  The recent KelpDAO hack underscores that challenge. While the attack was not linked to a flaw in audited smart contract code, users still saw another crypto platform lose funds. Security experts say most investors do not distinguish between a coding failure and an operational failure when money is lost.  According to the report, reducing those risks will require more than code reviews. Researchers said projects should strengthen private key security, improve monitoring systems, expand employee security training, and add safeguards that can detect suspicious activity before losses escalate.  Related: SBF Says He Could Launch a New Coin After Prison as Lost Investments Reach Billions

‡  About Those Enhanced PPs...  In case you hadn't heard about this recently revealed issue, images of two sets of customized past performances were posted (one example is attached), and caused a stir because they had been "enhanced" with veterinary data. That data included notations of intra-articular injections, and shockwave therapy.  There are several problems with such data being found in customized PPs that are not available to the general public. The information is widely presumed to be private and proprietary. Veterinarians are not allowed to divulge such information without the consent of the owner or trainer of the horse in question, nor should anyone else, even given "authorized" access to a related database.  There has been speculation online about related questions, some thoughtful, and predictably, some reckless. I'll review what I consider to be a few of the main questions, and provide my thoughts on the most likely answers, based on what has come to light thus far.  • Who were/are the consumers of the custom PPs?  It seems most likely that they were provided, or sold to at least one CAW entity. CAWs have more resources and power than individual consumers, and the PPs were enhanced with more than veterinary information, including multiple speed figures (i.e. Ragozin and Beyer), workout grades, and pace (or race shape) figures, etc. If whoever produced them did not have a private, well-heeled, and discreet audience, it would be difficult to imagine that they could have remained under the radar for any significant length of time.  • From what database was the veterinary information in the custom PPs sourced? Given the information that has surfaced, it appears highly likely that The Jockey Club's InCompass database was the source. There are several supporting reasons to arrive at that conclusion. Thanks to @DougieSal, a former Racing Secretary who has provided some useful insights into related questions, we know that there is a clue in the PPs themselves. Note that there are two associated dates listed with the injection notations. The first, on the far left, is presumably the date of the treatment, and placement of the horse on a "vet's list" and the second is denoted by the phrase "off date". The latter is a references to the date on which the horse was taken "off" of a vet's list, and therefore became eligible to race again.    The above referenced Doug Salvatore revealed an important insight, which is that those who work in racing offices not only have access to the InCompass database, but when checking eligibility, they are apparently able to see the reason(s) why horses were placed on the vet's lists. I strongly suspect that racing office employees are able to view that information for all horses at all racetracks, as, among other possible reasons, they would need to check the data on incoming shippers.    Doug produced a useful video breakdown of the PPs that can be accessed through this link: nitter.privacyredirect.com/DougieSal/status/20664…     Lisa Lazarus, the CEO of HISA, commented on the issue this week. She was quoted in TDN as saying: "the screenshots of PPs circulating online could not have come from the HISA Portal because some of the information is different from what is in the HISA Portal."     That isn't fully compelling, but she did elaborate on the @Steve_Byk Tuesday podcast (available through his archive). She pointed out that the InCompass system, which has been in place since well before the creation of HISA, includes basic veterinary inputs, while HISA's database includes much more granular detail. She also made the important distinction that the HISA records are accessible only to a very narrow group of authorized people, including the vets, owners and trainers associated with individual horses. Presumably regulatory vets do have access to all of the horses in the database, but it is safe to assume that access is vastly more restrictive than on the InCompass database.    I would argue that it is highly likely that the InCompass database is the source of the subject information, because the (limited) published information corresponds closely to what is available on that system, and the database is much easier to access than the HISA database.   • Who sourced, or leaked the veterinary information that was used in the custom PPs? This is the most difficult question to answer, not least of which because those who may be currently investigating won't be providing any clues. Given the tiny sample (two sets from NY-based horses), we don't even know with certainty that the (custom) PPs included information on all horses racing in the U.S. But if it were limited to a single racetrack or circuit, it would obviously be of limited value, and if, as suggested above, the InCompass system is not compartmentalized (i.e. the full database is available to any "authorized" user, no matter where they are located), then it would make no sense to limit the scope of the potentially valuable information that was being skimmed.    Even if we were to assume that the information came from InCompass, and that it was being provided to a CAW "team", there are questions that make homing in on a leaker difficult. I think it's fair to assume that the veterinary information was gathered en masse, or in computer lingo, "batch processing" was used, which is coincidentally how CAWs place large numbers of wagers efficiently. But even given a relatively efficient process, the data must have been skimmed and updated regularly, and somehow transferred to the database used by whoever constructs the PPs. Perhaps it is not a terribly sophisticated process these days, but superficially it would seem to me that it would still be much easier to accomplish if someone had truly unfettered access, different from that of a low-level racing official working at a desk in a racing office. Those with serious tech chops are welcome to chime in on the topic.    Could it have been a remote "hack"? It's possible, I suppose, but that would seem unnecessary if someone who was authorized to access InCompass was able to get the job done without triggering any alarms.    One clue that a reader might be able to provide is whether the type of (embedded) custom PPs are associated with any particular CAW team. In other words, setting aside the vet info, does anyone recognize the basic style of those PPs? And if so, where have you seen them previously?  • Solutions Moving Forward While I am entirely sympathetic to retail customers (i.e. bettors) who believe that such information is valuable, and should be available to all, I don't think that it's realistic to imagine that it might happen. Why? Because those with the greatest wealth and power in the industry, particularly major breeding farms, would be fiercely opposed to such disclosures. They want their valuable stallion prospects to appear as untainted as possible, and any stain, such as a history of joint injections, could adversely affect the bottom-line.    The good news, however, is that there does appear to be a fairly straightforward solution to the problem of CAWs (or anyone else) gaining privileged access to this type of data, at least through the InCompass system. I have no idea why anyone might have thought that it was originally a good idea to reveal specific vet treatment info to racing office personnel. After all, the only crucial bit of information is whether a given horse is eligible to race or not, and in the context of a vet's list, the only thing that a racing official should need to know is whether the horse is on or off the list.    Would it be difficult for The Jockey Club to separate the veterinary treatment details from simple on/off list data that could be viewed by racing office personnel? I don't see why it should be, nor am I aware of any compelling reason why those in racing offices might have any need to see treatment details.    Of course closing a possible InCompass loophole wouldn't insure that similar leaks could not occur, but at the very least it would reduce the likelihood, and significantly reduce the field of possible suspects in the case of any future leaks.  • Lingering Questions We've heard from HISA's CEO on the matter, but when might we hear from the Jockey Club? Is the latter investigating the possibility, or likelihood that InCompass is the source of the vet data leak? Does their system allow them to identify batch downloads of specific data? Are they considering the type of restrictions suggested above, in order to plug a possible leak? Will the person(s) who accessed and leaked the data ultimately be identified and punished?  • Final Thoughts Data protection is an increasingly big issue in today's world. Typically, concerns revolve around the unauthorized access of personal details, and this issue includes an (equine) element of that. But it also further irritates an already very sore subject, namely that the racing industry's retail customers have been increasingly abused over recent decades. The most recent abuse has been the explosive growth of CAWs, and advantages given to them (e.g. far lower effective takeout rates). That some of them may have also benefitted from potentially valuable, illicitly attained information, is the very definition of pouring salt on a wound.     There are easily identifiable reasons why wagering handle has plummeted >50% over the past 20 years (inflation adjusted) in the U.S. And while neither HISA nor The Jockey Club are designed to focus on wagering issues, the security of their databases is indirectly important to the industry's retail customers, as this issue illustrates. So it would be nice if the latter, whose database appears most likely to be the source of the information, were to respond as if it understands the importance of that underlying connection.

sample_tweets.text: Russia doesn't improvise. It runs a system, refined over decades, applied across every available vector simultaneously.  533 documented incidents in our archive. Date span from March 1982 through June 2026. That's not a campaign. That's a doctrine.  ---  THE METHODS  Disinformation is the core instrument. 245 incidents, nearly half the total. Russia builds false narratives the way engineers build infrastructure: load-bearing, redundant, designed to outlast the correction. The goal usually isn't to convince anyone of a specific lie. It's to corrode the baseline trust that makes shared political reality possible.  Election interference: 115 incidents. From the cold war-era playbook of backing sympathetic parties with covert funding to the contemporary model of social media amplification, voter file intrusions, and strategic leak operations. Hungary's 2026 parliamentary elections are the current live case. Russia has strong incentives to keep Orbán's Fidesz in power given what a Fidesz loss would mean for NATO and EU Russia policy. The interference environment around that election is documented as multi-directional and high-stakes.  Cyber espionage: 111 incidents. This is the collection arm. Universities, government ministries, defense contractors, research institutions. Ireland moved in June 2026 to build a formal institutional framework protecting universities from exactly this, joining a widening group of Western democracies that have concluded informal guidance isn't sufficient anymore. The Five Eyes warnings driving that move have been consistent and increasingly specific.  Social media hijacking: 93 incidents. Not just bot networks amplifying content. Coordinated inauthentic behavior at scale, persona management, hijacking of genuine grassroots movements, insertion of Russian-origin narratives into organic domestic debates. The platform problem here is structural: the same features that make social media useful for organizing make it trivially easy to poison.  State media coordination: 78 incidents. RT, Sputnik, and the broader ecosystem of laundering outlets and useful aggregators. The coordination with overt state media gives disinformation operations a citation layer. A rumor seeded on social media gets picked up by a nominally independent outlet, cited by RT, and can now be referenced as a "report." The Small Wars Journal analysis documented this mechanics specifically in June 2026, calling it weaponized uncertainty applied to the information battlespace.  Infrastructure attacks: 51 incidents. Power grids, pipelines, logistics networks. Mostly but not exclusively in Eastern Europe and the former Soviet space. The intent is rarely purely destructive. It's coercive. Demonstrating reach and imposing costs.  Advanced Persistent Threat operations: 45 incidents. Long-dwell network intrusions. APT28, APT29, Sandworm. Patient access over months or years, positioned for collection or disruption depending on what the political calendar requires.  Influence operations as a discrete category: 41 incidents. Overlaps with the disinformation count but captures the broader category of perception management, front organizations, cultivated assets, and the kind of long-running narrative infrastructure that doesn't always produce a specific incident but shapes the environment for when one is needed.  ---  MARQUEE CASES  The UK conviction in June 2026 is the cleanest recent illustration of how the model works. Two individuals convicted in a Russian-directed plot against the British PM. The plan combined arson attacks as physical sabotage with a false-flag disinformation campaign designed to attribute the attacks to Ukrainian nationals. A Russian diplomat was identified as the alleged handler. That combination, sabotage plus a pre-built cover narrative, is not improvisation. That's rehearsed. The false-flag layer matters as much as the physical operation, maybe more.  The 2026 Worldwide Threats Hearing put Russia explicitly in a tri-adversary framework alongside China and Iran, describing complementary interference strategies designed to undermine U.S. democratic institutions. The specific concern in the hearing documentation: weakened domestic counter-interference capabilities arriving just before the November 2026 midterms. That timing is not coincidental from the Russian side. Operations are calibrated to electoral cycles.  The European Parliament resolution on transnational repression in June 2026 targeted the coercive operations Russia runs against diaspora communities inside EU member states. Exiled critics, foreign-based journalists, opposition figures. The resolution called for dismantling the coercive networks, which is an acknowledgment that those networks are real and currently operational.  ---  WHAT TO WATCH  The hybrid model is the thing. Russia doesn't run a cyber operation or a disinformation operation or a sabotage operation. It runs all of them in coordination, often with each providing cover or amplification for the others. The UK case is a clean example. The Hungary election environment is another: interference documented from multiple directions simultaneously, with the Russian incentive structure clearly mapped.  The academic and research sector is an expanding front. Ireland joining the growing list of countries building formal counter-espionage frameworks for universities is a signal of how seriously Five Eyes is taking this. Research institutions hold dual-use technical knowledge, pre-publication data, and relationships with government and defense adjacent communities. They're softer targets than ministries, and Russia has been systematic about it.  The diaspora targeting is underreported relative to its severity. Transnational repression is resource-intensive. You don't run coercive operations against exiles in multiple EU countries without institutional commitment. The European Parliament resolution is a measure of how widespread the problem has become.  533 incidents over 44 years. The pattern is stable because the doctrine is stable. Disruption, confusion, erosion of trust. Applied everywhere, constantly, with patience. The specific targets shift. The method doesn't.  foreigninterference.org #foreigninterference #DisinformationCampaigns #ElectionInterference #CyberEspionage #SocialMediaHijacking

The NCSC's advisory board is running six active items today, and the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN entry is the one that earns the red badge — meaning exploitation confirmed in the wild, not "patch before attackers find it." The Register reported this morning that Cisco expanded the scope of that same max-severity bulletin, adding another SD-WAN device variant after initial publication. A living advisory, not a static one.  That expansion is also the likely explanation for an earlier pipeline classification failure that flagged as "unknown." NCSC-native advisories without a clean NVD CVE anchor at time of publication can surface in ingestion feeds without the metadata to label them correctly. The signal was real. The label wasn't. Not a gap in coverage — a gap in the taxonomy.  The rest of the board deserves attention in roughly this order:  The Citrix NetScaler double-header — CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368 — has been sitting at "immediate action requested" since March 25. The clock has been running for three months. NetScaler Gateway is a standard enterprise perimeter product, and unpatched instances at this point are simply exposed. We are nothing if not consistent.  The APT28 router DNS hijacking advisory (April 7, Five Eyes joint publication) is the most geopolitically significant item on the board. Russian state actor. Network infrastructure targeting. Adversary-in-the-middle credential harvest. The reason this one has long-tail risk that a simple patch doesn't resolve: the attack vector is router compromise, not a patchable software flaw. Compromised DNS state requires active forensic investigation. Patch status is not the right question here.  F5 BIG-IP APM unauthenticated RCE rounds out the high-severity tier, published March 30, patch encouraged, still active.  The meta-context for all six is the NCSC's own framing from May 1 — CTO Ollie Whitehouse's "patch wave" advisory, which put on record that frontier AI models are now accelerating vulnerability discovery faster than most organizations can patch. These six advisories are not independent incidents. They are the current leading edge of that wave.  If Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN, Citrix NetScaler, or F5 BIG-IP APM are anywhere in the stack — the Cisco item is active-exploitation priority, not patch-queue. The APT28 advisory requires a router audit regardless of patch status. The rest of the board is not theoretical either.

Macron et l’ANSSI ont bien attribué des opérations sophistiquées (espionnage, sabotage hybride) à des groupes liés au GRU russe, comme APT28, sur des hôpitaux et infrastructures depuis 2021. C’est documenté, pas une invention. Oui, il y a eu des arrestations d’Ukrainiens (affiliés à LockBit par exemple en juillet 2025), mais avec la coopération des autorités de Kyiv. Ces gars sont des criminels motivés par l’argent (rançongiciels), pas des agents d’État. LockBit est un groupe russophone, et ces arrestations ne disculpent pas la Russie dans les attaques étatiques. La cybermenace est réelle et double : criminelle + hybride russe.

sample_tweets.text: 교묘해진 신종 보이스피싱의 덫  1. 청년들이 '성실하게' 빚 1억을 지고 사회에 나오는 구조 많은 기성세대는 청년들이 사치나 과소비 때문에 파산한다고 오해하지만, 실상은 학교만 착실히 다녀도 빚더미에 앉는 구조입니다.  - 학자금 및 생활비 대출 한 학기 등록금과 기본 생활비 대출을 받으면 매 학기 약 1,000만 원, 8학기 졸업 시 기본 8,000만 원의 빚이 생깁니다.  - 서울 대학생 숨만 쉬어도 월 200만 원 서울 월세(하숙·고시원도 70~80만 원), 통신비, 하루 최소 2~3만 원의 식비 등을 더하면 아무것도 안 하고 숨만 쉬어도 월 200~250만 원의 생활비가 고스란히 깨집니다.   부모의 전폭적인 지원이 없다면 알바로 메우기 불가능해 졸업 시점에 이미 1억~1억 5천만 원 안팎의 부채를 안고 사회에 첫발을 내딛게 됩니다.  - 상환 불가능한 중위소득 중소기업에 취업해 월 300만 원(청년층 중위소득 수준)을 벌어도 기본 생활비를 쓰고 나면 원금은커녕 대출 이자(5% 기준 연 500만 원)만 겨우 감당하는 수준에 그칩니다.   여기에 실직이나 보이스피싱 같은 작은 트리거 하나만 터지면 리볼빙과 카드 돌려막기로 이어지며 순식간에 파산으로 직행합니다.  2. 돈을 준다고 유혹하는 신종 보이스피싱의 늪 과거 검찰 사칭이나 돈을 요구하는 방식과 달리, 요즘 젊은 층을 노리는 보이스피싱은 "돈(계약금·상금)을 주겠다"며 접근하여 심리적으로 완전히 종속시킵니다.  - SNS 모델 사칭 사기 사례 인스타그램 DM으로 "미국 브랜드 한국 런칭 모델로 선정되어 계약금 1억 원을 주겠다"고 현혹합니다.   이후 "미국 모델 협회 등록비 15만 원", "미국 계좌 개설비 50만 원", "불법 체류 방지 보증금" 등 소액부터 시작해 요구 액수를 서서히 늘려갑니다 (심리학의 '문간에 발 들여놓기' 기법).  - 매몰 비용의 함정과 파멸 피해자는 1억을 받을 기대감과 이미 넣은 돈 아까운 '매몰 비용' 심리 때문에 의심하면서도 대출을 받아 결국 7,000만 원이 넘는 거액을 송금하게 됩니다. 대개 "더 이상 넣을 돈이 없을 때" 비로소 사기임을 깨닫게 됩니다.  - 피해자를 수거책으로 만드는 2차 사기 사기를 인지한 피해자에게 가짜 변호사·해커가 접근해 착수금을 또 뜯어내고, 대출을 알선해 주겠다며 통장 비밀번호를 요구해 대포통장 명의자로 만들거나 아르바이트를 빙자해 보이스피싱 수거책(범죄자)으로 전락시키는 잔인한 올인원(All-in-one) 조직 범죄가 횡행하고 있습니다.  3. 실패할 권리가 없는 한국 사회 vs 미국 트럼프의 차이 - 미국과 유럽의 파산 인식 도널드 트럼프는 본인 기업을 4~6번 파산시키고도 대통령이 되었고, 에디슨, 헨리 포드, 록펠러 등 위대한 혁신가들도 모두 파산 경험이 있습니다.   자본주의의 핵심인 '유한책임'을 인정하고 은행의 이자는 '원금을 떼일 리스크에 대한 보험료'로 보기 때문에 실패에 너그럽습니다.  - 한국의 가혹한 채무자 낙인 반면 한국은 대부업체나 은행이 채무자의 숨통이 끊어질 때까지 평생 추심하고 소멸시효를 기계적으로 연장하며 급여를 압류합니다.   한 번 실패하면 재기가 불가능한 사회적 환경 때문에, 청년들은 평생 빚만 갚다 끝나느니 1%의 확률에 목숨을 걸고 선물옵션, 코인, 잡코인 등 극단적인 도박성 투재(한탕주의)에 빠져들게 됩니다.  4. 은행의 무책임한 지대 추구와 보안 불감증 - 담보 대출만 하는 한국 은행 대한민국 은행들은 인물이나 기술 아이템의 가능성을 보고 대출해 주는 예가 전혀 없으며, 오직 부동산 담보 대출과 국가가 보증 서주는 기금 대출만 받아 가며 예대마진으로 영업이익 40조 원을 손쉽게 벌어들입니다.  - 보안 투자 턱없이 부족 미국의 JP모건 체이스는 해킹 및 금융 사기를 막기 위해 연간 순이익의 23%인 20조 원을 보안에 투자하는 반면, 국내 리딩뱅크인 국민은행은 순이익의 고작 1% 남짓한 400억 원 수준만 보안에 씁니다.   법과 판례가 사기를 당하면 '당한 놈이 바보'라며 금융 소비자에게 책임을 전가하고 규제만 피하면 은행에 면책을 주기 때문에 보안 시스템을 강화하지 않는 것입니다. 은행이 해킹과 금융 사기 피해를 책임지도록 법을 바꾸면 사기 범죄는 즉각 차단될 것입니다.  5. 재기의 사다리를 위한 실질적인 정책 제안 - 개인회생 변호사 비용 '클린 바우처' 도입 개인회생 제도는 국가가 성실한 채무자를 구제하기 위해 마련한 좋은 제도이지만 서류가 복잡해 개인이 하긴 불가능합니다. 정작 돈이 없어 회생을 못 하는 청년들이 사채업자가 결탁한 불법 수임료 대출 브로커의 늪에 빠집니다.   정부(허그, 신보 등)가 회생 신청자에게 200만 원 상당의 변호사 수임료 바우처를 빌려주고, 이 비용을 회생 절차에서 최우선으로 상환받는 '우선변제채권'으로 지정하면 국가 예산을 낭비하지 않고도 나쁜 브로커를 퇴출하고 청년들을 양성화할 수 있습니다.  - 자가 채무진단 플랫폼 및 익명 데이터 개방 개인회생, 일반회생, 신용회복위원회 워크아웃 등 일반인이 알기 힘든 수많은 채무조정 제도 중 나에게 맞는 제도를 MBTI 검사처럼 쉽게 찾아주는 국가 차원의 진단 사이트와 익명 통계 API 개방이 시급합니다.  💡 실패한 청년들이 노숙인이나 범죄 수거책으로 전락하게 두는 것은 국가적으로 생산·소비 인구를 모두 잃는 자멸의 길입니다. 급변하는 AI 시대에는 누구나 순식간에 빚더미에 앉을 수 있는 만큼, 회생·파산 제도를 단순한 도덕적 해이로 손가락질할 것이 아니라 사회의 가장 밑바닥을 지탱하는 '필수적인 사회적 안전망'으로 인정하고 활성화해야 국가가 생존할 수 있습니다.

인용 들 보니 왜 이 의견을 안사는 놈이라 내고있을거라 믿는 사람들이 있나 모르겠네...  과거에(20년 넘었네요) 해적질 하다 구매자가 된 사람이 두 상황을 모두 보며 이야기하고 있을거라는 가능성을 배제하면 안됩니다.  대한민국은 2000년대 까지만 해도 해적들이 주류였던 시장인걸요. 중간에 구매자로 전향한 사람들은 어떤 일들이 있고 살아남은 시장들이 어떻게 구축되어 왔는지 다 봐왔어요.  한예로 PC게임 시장이 저 생각을 하던 적이 있었는데,(다운수x순이익 으로 계산하고 기사내곤 했습니다.) 결국 '문제가 된건 경로가 아니라 사람들의 분위기와 문화, 경제력 등의 복합적 문제였다'는 결론을 마주한채 몰락하고,   그 문제가 해결되고 나서도 '스팀에서 게임사서 인증하기'가 유행이 된 이후에야 간신히 살아난 적이 있습니다. 스팀에서 세일 중인 게임 몇 개씩 사서 인증글 올리면 반응 쩌는데 다들 따라했죠. 근데 사서하니 예전에 복돌이짓 할때 보다 X나 편하네? 전향이 시작됐습니다.  이거 조금만 생각해보면 돼요. 대한민국 국민 절대 다수가 뭐가 문제 있어서 화당 천원도 안될 웹툰, 웹소를 안사주겠냐는거죠.  그러면 '거기에 돈 쓰고싶지 않아'+'지금 보는건 무료라서 그래'라는 기업이랑 창작다 입장에서는 별로 보고싶지 않은 교집합의 가능성이 굉장히 높다는 결룐이 나와버립니다.  그래서 서구권에서 한때 시도되고, 지금도 남은 전략이 구매자를 더 챙겨주는건데 현 한국 웹툰, 웹소 시장의 화당 단가로 뭘 더 챙겨줄 여력은 없죠.  유료구매자 미리보기 형태로 단가 내에서 해줄건 다 해주고 있기도 하고. 편의성이나 가격 면에서 접근 장벽이랄건 사실상 없어요.  이 정도로 편의 봐주고 단가 낮게 유지하는데도 안오는건 그냥 돈 안쓸 사람이라고 보는게 맞습니다...  굳이 따지자면 불법복제 사이트에 해킹 툴 등이 심어져있을 가능성이나(이것도 모바일로 보면 보통 막히죠), 저급한 광고가 다량 나오는게 그나마 합법 시장의 공세 포인트가 될겁니다.  '어차피 똥같은 내용의 광고 보며 볼거면 멀쩡한 광고 나오는 우리한테서 봐라. 광고 티어를 새로 개설할거다!'  그리고 보통 불법 사이트들은 서버비랑 운영비를 화당 결제 안하고 광고로 전부 수급해야해서  광고 미친듯이 붙이니 생각보다 많이 달아놔도 볼걸요? (조회수 보면 트래픽 부하 터지는 중일거야 불보듯 뻔하죠)  그래도 안오면 뭘 해도 안볼 사람이니 포기하는게 빠를거에요.  예를들어 왜 불법사이트서 보냐고 했더니 '여기서 보면 플랫폼 와리가리 안하고 한 곳에서 다 봐서 좋아'같은 합법시장에서는 도저히 불가능한 니즈 기반으로 보는 사람이라던가... 이런 경우 구매자로 돌아설 가능성은 나락으로 떨어지니까요.
이것도 중국 해커들인가요?  도대체 우리나라는 중국 해커들 '어서오십시오'하는건가요?  이번 앤트로픽의 한국 통신사를 통한 중국 해킹 이슈도 마찬가지 입니다.  미국이 우방국들에게 극비로 권한 준 것을 이렇게 중국에 털리는 건 심각한 문제입니다.

현재 수사를 못했다 뿐이지 북한과 중국은 한국 선거에 충분히 관여할 수 있습니다. 2개만 말해볼게요  1. 미친 선관위 서버 2023년 북한 해킹 공격에 선관위도 포함되어있었고 선관위는 본인들이 해킹 당한줄로 몰랐습니다. 이후 진행된 국정원의 조사결과 선관위서버는 해킹에 취약하고 해당 서버에 해킹해 1) 선거인명부조작 2) 투개표율 조작  3) 가짜 사전투표지 출력가능  이 3가지가 모두 가능하다는걸 밝혀냈습니다. 국정원 사이트에 보도자료가 올라와 있으니 보시죠.  2. 중국인도 투표 가능한 지선  지선은 외국인 투표가 가능하고 우리나라 외국인 유권자 78퍼가 중국인 입니다. 심지어 한국에 안살아도 투표 가능해요. 중국인이 투표권을 행사한다면 친중국적 정당을 뽑겠죠? 뭐 재외투표나 사전투표는 신분증명이 널럴하다 못해 뻥 뚤려있으니 위조신분증으로 불법체류자가 충분히  투표할 수 있습니다.   중국은 이미 미국, 호주, 일본, 독일, 필리핀 선거에 개입한 전적이 있고 미국과 필리핀에서는 중국 스파이가 당선도 되었죠. 그런데 과연 옆나라 한국 선거에 개입안했을까요? 특히 한국어/중국어 모두 구사가 가능한 조선족이 넘치는 이나라에서?  중국/북한 선거개입은 매우가능한  시나리오입니다.

🍄 곽튜브가 코인 사기범 ???? 역대급 도용 피해… 곽튜브가 코인 사기범으로 오인당했다.  구독자 214만 유튜버 곽튜브(곽준빈)이 글로벌 가상자산 해킹 사태의 핵심 인물로 잘못 지목당하는 황당한 피해를 입었다. 최근 가상자산 프로젝트 ‘휴머니티 프로토콜’이 해킹당해 약 544억원 규모 피해가 발생했다.  그러자 CEO 테렌스 곽이 자신의 X 프로필 사진을 곽튜브 사진으로 바꾸고 잠적했다. 해외 투자자들은 곽튜브를 테렌스 곽으로 착각하고  “수백억 코인 사기꾼”이라며 대대적인 비난을 퍼부었다. 곽튜브는 SNS에 직접 밝혔다.  “나는 한국 유튜버다. 코인은 시도조차 한 적 없다.  내 사진을 훔쳤다.” 살다 살다 코인 사기 도용까지 당하는 상황…  곽튜브에게 피해가 고스란히 돌아갔다. (현재 상황 파악 중) 이런 사진·신원 도용으로 피해를 보는 사람이 더 이상 없어야 합니다. 디지털 시대에 타인의 얼굴과 이름을 함부로 쓰는 행위는 심각한 범죄입니다. 빠른 해결을 바랍니다 .

sample_tweets.text: Yo I'm about to perform my first cyber attack

@NCSC IT'S BUGDROID AND NATO 2010 AND 1997 WE NEED YOU TO DISABLE GRAPHICS ACCELERATION ENTIRELY TO STOP THE CRIMINALS, INVISIBLE THREAT VECTOR NEEDING ECC RAM TO FIX ON GPU.  Overview of the FortiBleed Campaign  Threat Actor Profile and Discovery  The large-scale cyber espionage and initial access campaign widely referred to as "FortiBleed" represents an exceptionally organized, industrial-scale credential harvesting operation. Orchestrated by a multi-operator, Russian-speaking cybercriminal syndicate, the operation was uncovered when security researcher Volodymyr "Bob" Diachenko discovered the attackers' command-and-control server accidentally left exposed to the open internet. The unsecured staging server contained an expansive array of operational tools, automated exploitation scripts, detailed attack logs, and a verified database of working credentials categorized systematically for monetization on the dark web.  Unprecedented Scale of Compromise  Unlike typical isolated breaches, FortiBleed functions as a massive infrastructure dragnet.  Global Footprint: The compromised dataset explicitly identifies working administrative and SSL VPN login credentials for 73,932 to 75,000 unique FortiGate firewall URLs. Domain Penetration: The scope spans across 194 countries and impacts over 21,632 distinct corporate and institutional domains. Internet-Facing Density: Network telemetry verified by independent researchers indicates that this single dataset comprises roughly 50% of the entire global volume of internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls visible via public scanners like Shodan.  Technical Analysis of the Attack Chain  [Internet-Facing FortiGate Management Interface]                      │          (1.16 Billion Credential Sweeps)                      ▼        [Configuration File Extraction]                      │        (Legacy SHA-256 Hashing Explored)                      ▼      [Offline 45-GPU Hashtopolis Cluster]                      │          (Admin Access & Traffic Sniffing)                      ▼ [Lateral Movement to Internal Active Directory]  Phase 1: Reconnaissance and Mass Scanning The intrusion cycle begins with automated, high-velocity internet sweeps designed to isolate any perimeter hardware exposing its FortiGate Management Interface directly to the public web. Once identified, the threat actors initiate massive credential-stuffing campaigns. The operational scale of this phase is immense: the actors launched an estimated 1.16 billion authentication attempts against more than 320,000 unique FortiGate targets. This initial automation relies heavily on massive identity dumps aggregated from old data breaches and active infostealer malware logs, matching saved browser and VPN client passwords against the target portals. Concurrently, the group ran 2.1 billion brute-force attempts targeting over 160,000 internet-exposed MSSQL servers globally. Phase 2: Configuration Extraction and Offline Hashing Exploitation Upon gaining an initial foothold through credential reuse or the exploitation of unpatched vulnerabilities, the threat actors systematically download the device configuration files. These configuration exports contain the cryptographic hashes of administrative and VPN user passwords. The primary catalyst for the campaign's success is a specific weakness in credential storage legacy formats. Although Fortinet introduced firmware updates in early 2025 that upgraded administrative credential storage to the computationally expensive PBKDF2 hashing standard, this cryptographic protection is only applied if an administrator actively logs back into the device after the firmware update has been completed. Consequently, tens of thousands of devices that were successfully patched against older exploits still retained internal configuration files storing credentials in the weaker, legacy SHA-256 with Salt format. The threat actors pulled these legacy hashes offline and processed them through a dedicated, high-performance 45-GPU cluster managed via Hashtopolis. Because the cracking was performed entirely offline with massive computing power, standard password complexity controls offered virtually zero protection to affected organizations. Phase 3: Post-Compromise Pivoting and Lateral Movement Once the plaintext credentials are mathematically recovered, the operators log directly into the firewall management console with root-level privileges. The compromised perimeter appliance is then weaponized internally. The actors intercept ongoing SSL VPN sessions, capture real-time authentication traffic, and gather additional corporate identity tokens. Using the firewall as an encrypted staging point inside the perimeter, the threat actors bypass external security controls and execute deep lateral movement into internal segments, focusing specifically on compromising Active Directory environments to gain full domain control. Detailed Impact Metrics The leaked database outlines targeted exposure across multinational enterprises, critical infrastructure, and government nodes. The underlying data logs explicitly categorize victims by company name, estimated corporate revenue, industry sector, and country. Geographic Dispersion (Top Affected Countries) CountryCompromised Device VolumeIndia9,629United States6,352Taiwan3,637Mexico3,197Turkey3,032Thailand2,939  Sector Penetration (Top Affected Verticals)  Industry SectorDocumented CompromisesIT Services1,975Telecommunications574Industrial Equipment & OT467Financial Services460Government Services454Healthcare365  Critical Security Warning: High-profile entities identified within the threat actor's active validation logs include multinational organizations such as Foxconn, Samsung, Comcast, Siemens, Lenovo, PwC, Accenture, and Oracle. Multiple full network takeovers have been confirmed, including an intrusion into a Turkish NATO defense contractor that resulted in the successful exfiltration of classified defense documentation.  Comprehensive Remediation and Mitigation Strategy  Because FortiBleed represents a widespread credential compromise and data reuse campaign rather than a distinct, unpatched software vulnerability, traditional patching alone will not evict attackers if the active administrative keys have already been harvested. Security teams must execute a structured, multi-phase defense protocol:  1. Perimeter Hardening  Eliminate Public Management Access: Immediately modify firewall policies to remove the FortiGate/FortiOS Management Interface from the public, internet-facing perimeter. Limit administrative access strictly to internal, trusted local area network (LAN) segments or dedicated out-of-band management networks.  Exposed URL Audit: Verify the outward attack surface using external network scanners to ensure that SSL VPN portals are isolated from administrative login pathways.  2. Credential Invalidation and Forced Re-Hashing  Global Administrative Rotation: Force an immediate, mandatory password rotation for all administrative accounts, local system accounts, and SSL VPN user profiles associated with Fortinet appliances.  Trigger PBKDF2 Migration: Upgrade all firewalls to the latest available FortiOS firmware version. Following the upgrade, mandate that every single administrator execute a fresh, manual login sequence. This active authentication step is mathematically required to force FortiOS to purge legacy SHA-256 hashes from the system configuration file and re-encrypt the credentials using the highly secure PBKDF2 protocol.  3. Identity and Authentication Enhancements  Enforce Strict MFA: Deploy mandatory Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) across all administrative management portals and all external SSL VPN entry nodes without exception.  Zero Trust Access: Transition remote administrative workflows toward Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) frameworks to minimize reliance on static perimeter credentials.  4. Incident Response and Threat Hunting Hunt for Persistence Prior to Lockout: Review internal system configuration states for unauthorized secondary accounts, modified API keys, or rogue SSH keys before rotating passwords, as threat actors frequently drop auxiliary access vectors once inside. Audit Active Directory Logs: Cross-reference firewall connection logs with internal Active Directory domain controller security event logs. Inspect internal networks for "Living off the Land" techniques, focusing on unexpected administrative tool executions, anomalous internal lateral connections, or unauthorized attempts to dump NTDS.dit files. Verify Exposure Databases: Security operations centers should leverage authorized threat intelligence lookup portals, such as the verified repositories managed by Hudson Rock, to explicitly check if their corporate firewall URLs or domains are present in the recovered FortiBleed adversary database.

🚨 CYBER INTELLIGENCE ALERT: ALLEGED COMPROMISE OF HEALTH INFRASTRUCTURE AND EXFILTRATION — ARGENTINA 🇦🇷  [STATUS: THREAT UNDER INVESTIGATION / UNCONFIRMED / EXFILTRATION OF HEALTH DATA / SOURCE: SOCIAL MEDIA]  SOULHEM TEAM AWARDS ACQUISITION OF 52 MILLION DATA RECORDS FROM THE MINISTRY OF HEALTH AND ANSES RECORDS  The threat actor identified by the alias Sqx, operating on behalf of the cybercriminal collective Soulhem Team, has announced the alleged intrusion and compromise of the core systems of the Argentine Ministry of Health.  The attacker claims to have exfiltrated a massive volume totaling 52 million records, including databases of healthcare professionals, beneficiary files, and documents cross-referenced with the National Social Security Administration (ANSES).  🏢 Allegedly Affected Entities: Ministry of Health of the Argentine Republic and National Social Security Administration (ANSES).  👤 Threat Actors: Sqx / Soulhem Team  ⚔️ Potential Attack Vector: Extraction of databases by compromising credentials of internal vaccination management platforms, public sector payroll portals, or national health interoperability APIs.  🔍 Verification Status: SUSPECTED / UNCONFIRMED. The extent of the breach and the direct impact on the ministries' central servers remain under strict audit by the State's cybersecurity agencies.  ⚠️ CRITICAL RISK ANALYSIS AND DECLARED FIELDS  If the validity of the 52 million data lines is confirmed, the criminal impact and the effects on citizens and public institutions introduce severe risk scenarios:  👤 Exposure of Personally Identifiable Information (PII) of Physicians: The attacker claims to possess the Full Name, National Identity Document (DNI), Gmail Address, Gender, Postal Code, and Date of Entry of every healthcare professional in the country. This represents a clean database for mass identity theft campaigns, professional impersonation fraud, or targeted extortion.  🏥 Leakage of Medical Records and Patient Data: The exposed lists reveal patients' full names directly linked to their national identity card numbers (e.g., Vallejos Valentina Antonela, Quintana Yésica Belén, Santa Cruz Oscar Aníbal, Acevedo Celina Yamila), connecting them to reasons for emergency room visits and specific medical treatments. This breach of confidential health information directly violates national personal data protection laws.  🛡️ TECHNICAL RECOMMENDATIONS AND PREVENTIVE MITIGATION  🛑 Isolation and Forensic Investigation of Public Sector Networks (Immediate Corporate Action): The National Security Incident Response Teams (CERT .ar) and the IT administrators of the Ministry of Health are urged to audit the export logs of outpatient consultation and vaccination records systems, temporarily isolating any local hospital-level user accounts (such as those in Colón or Lanús) that show an anomalous volume of consecutive requests during the first half of 2026.  🔑 Hardening Access and Deployment of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): Restrict access to government portals for uploading medical data, forcing the expiration of all concurrent healthcare user sessions and mandating the use of robust authentication factors based on tokens or biometrics from the official application.  📊 MONITORING AND EVALUATION  Intelligence System:  analyzer.vecert.io  Quickly assess your website's security with:  monitor.vecert.io #CyberSecurity #Argentina #MinistryOfHealth #ANSES #SoulHem #SqxTeam #DataLeak #MedicalRecords #PIIExposure #ColonHospital #ThreatIntelligence #CyberAlert #VECERT #Infosec #UnverifiedBreach

When we first wrote this piece, Claude Mythos was at the center of cybersecurity discussions. Today, its future seems less certain.  But whether Mythos ultimately becomes widely available, remains tightly controlled, or is surpassed by the next generation of AI systems almost doesn't matter. What matters is what it represents.  The prospect of AI dramatically shrinking the time between vulnerability discovery and exploitation should be a wake-up call for defenders. Not because we're doomed, but because it exposes the limitations of security strategies that depend primarily on patching, detection, and response after the fact.  In that sense, Mythos could end up being a positive accelerator for the industry.  If organizations assume attackers can find and weaponize vulnerabilities faster than ever, the conversation naturally shifts toward fundamentals: segmentation, identity-based access controls, least privilege, and Zero Trust architectures that limit blast radius before an attack occurs.  It's time to implement preemptive security.  Rather than racing to catch every threat, it's about designing systems so that when threats inevitably emerge, they can't move freely, escalate privileges, or disrupt critical operations.  The organizations that embrace those fundamentals now will be far better positioned for an AI-accelerated threat landscape than those relying solely on faster patch cycles and better detection tools.  Worth a read if you're thinking about what AI-powered cyber capabilities mean for critical infrastructure and operational environments.  Read the blog: xage.com/blog/beyond-claude-… #ClaudeMythos #AIsecurity #Cybersecurity #ZeroTrust #CyberResilience #IdentitySecurity

‼️ WINDOWS CRYPTO USERS, BEWARE! (@MsftSecIntel)  There is a malware built to steal your crypto from the clipboard (CTRL + C or `copy` functions) in two ways:  1. replacing the copied address to an address controlled by the threat actor 2. sending copied seed phrases and private keys to the threat actor's server  ## EXPLANATION  On (1), you can still avoid losing money even if infected by this malware -- just double-check the address you are sending to  So, when you try to send transactions to alice by copying her 0x123alice...789 address, the script will change the data inside the windows clipboard to 0x123mallory...789 (mallory is the threat actor)  Double-checking protects you (and you should immediately disconnect from the internet and start scanning your device for the malware, or take it to someone who can help you getting rid of the threat)  (2) is self-explanatory: you copy your seed or private key and the attacker receives it in their machine, so they can later import into a wallet and drain your funds freely  Once you copied it, it's done. Not much you can do here  ## HOW TO SPOT THE MALWARE  Microsoft says one way to spot the malware is finding 4-6 random character folders in /Documents/ with .js and other unknown files and extensions  Be careful out there!  (and never, NEVER, insert unknown USB drivers and autorun USB files, as this is how you can get infected by this malware in particular)

Las amenazas dirigidas a los sistemas Linux no son tan frecuentes como las dirigidas a los sistemas Windows. Hay muchas razones para ello, pero normalmente la más sencilla es que el número de sistemas Windows utilizados como dispositivos personales o corporativos es mucho mayor que el de los de Linux, y eso por sí solo aumenta las probabilidades de éxito de cualquier ataque contra Windows. En el caso concreto de VoidLink, la característica más preocupante es su persistencia avanzada. Zero-days in endpoint security tools deserve immediate attention. Microsoft’s confirmation of the RoguePlanet Defender zero-day (CVE-2026-50656), a privilege escalation flaw, is a reminder that even trusted protection layers must be monitored, hardened, and updated as new threats emerge.  While a patch is under development, organizations should prioritize compensating controls:  - Monitor Microsoft guidance and apply the security update as soon as it is released - Review endpoints for unusual privilege escalation, Defender tampering, and suspicious process activity - Enforce least privilege and remove unnecessary local admin rights - Strengthen EDR/MDR monitoring and centralize logs for faster detection - Validate endpoint baselines, security policies, and tamper protection settings - Prepare response playbooks for privilege escalation incidents, including device isolation and credential rotation  𝗩𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺 𝗘𝗹𝗲𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗯𝘆 𝗩𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺𝗦𝗲𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗲𝗣𝗿𝗼 helps organizations reduce zero-day exposure, strengthen endpoint resilience, and improve detection with vCISO-led strategy, continuous monitoring, and measurable outcomes. S&P500 +1.08%(7,500.58), 나스닥 종합 +1.91%(26,517.93), 다우 +0.14%(51,564.70, +72p), 러셀2000 +2.12%(2,979.77). 전일(6/17) 케빈 워시 신임 의장의 매파적 FOMC 충격을 하루 만에 대부분 만회했다.  두 가지 핵심 동력이 충돌했다. ①트럼프의 인텔–애플 칩 협력 발표가 반도체를 폭등시켜 필라델피아 반도체지수(SOX)가 +6.67%(14,376.34) 급등 ②미–이란 양해각서(MOU) 서명과 호르무즈 봉쇄 해제로 유가가 급락(WTI ~$75, 3개월 최저)하며 에너지·방산주는 역으로 약세를 보였다.  리스크온 전환이 뚜렷했다. 지정학 리스크 완화로 변동성지수(VIX)가 약 -11% 급락(~16.4)하고, 소형주·시클리컬이 강세를 주도했다. 6월 19일(금)은 준틴스(Juneteenth) 휴장으로, 이날은 단축된 한 주의 마지막 거래일이었다.  Key Findings — 핵심 마감 수치  주요 지수 (6/18 종가)  지수 종가 등락률     S&P 500 7,500.58 +1.08%   나스닥 종합 26,517.93 +1.91%   다우존스 산업평균 51,564.70 +0.14% (+72p)   러셀 2000 2,979.77 +2.12% (지수 중 최고)   VIX 약 16.4 약 -11% (전일 18.44)   SOX(필라델피아 반도체) 14,376.34 +6.67% (+899.27p) 전일(6/17) 대비 기저효과가 컸다. 6/17에는 S&P -1.21%(7,420.10), 나스닥 -1.34%(26,021.66), 다우 -0.98%(51,492.55, -507p), VIX +12.37%(18.44)로 급락했었다.  장중 흐름  개장: 인텔–애플 뉴스와 칩 랠리로 S&P +0.9~1.2%, 나스닥 +1.6% 강하게 출발.  오후: 다우는 장중 약 +300p 올라 사상 최고치를 테스트했으나 상승폭을 반납해 +0.14%로 마감. 나스닥·러셀2000은 강세를 끝까지 유지하며 기술·소형주 주도 장세를 굳혔다.  섹터별 동향  강세: 기술·반도체(SOX +6.67%), AI 인프라(데이터센터·옵틱스·네트워킹), 소형 시클리컬. 나스닥100은 +2.3% 상승.  약세 — 에너지: S&P 에너지 섹터가 약 -2% 하락. 미–이란 MOU 서명과 유가 급락이 직격탄. 셰브론 -2.16%.  약세 — 방산: 전쟁 리스크 프리미엄 축소로 iShares 미국 항공우주·방산 ETF(ITA)가 약 -1.7%, 록히드마틴(LMT)도 하락. 다만 록히드는 2025년 말 기준 약 1,940억 달러 수주잔고와 F-35(2025년 191대 인도 기록) 등 다년 프로그램이 밸류에이션을 뒷받침해 구조적 투자논리는 유지된다는 평가.  다우 구성종목: 캐터필러 +3.67%, 홈디포 +2.83%, 3M +1.68% 상승 / IBM -5.43%, 세일즈포스 -2.73%, 셰브론 -2.16% 하락.  주요 개별 종목 (6/18)  반도체/AI (확정 종가)  종목 종가 등락률     NVIDIA (NVDA) $210.69 +2.95%   Micron (MU) $1,152.40 +1.62%   AMD $537.37 +4.86%   Broadcom (AVGO) $411.35 +4.70%   TSM (TSMC) $462.12 +6.94%   Nebius (NBIS) $286.69 +2.06% Intel (INTC): 약 +10.4% (약 $133) — 트럼프가 트루스소셜에 "애플이 인텔과 미국 내에서 칩을 설계·생산하기로 합의했다"고 발표한 것이 촉매. CBS는 INTC가 개장 직후 $133.82(+10.5%)까지 올랐다고 보도했고, 장중 사상 최고 $135.13(+12%)을 찍었다. 미 정부는 작년 8월 인텔 지분 약 10%를 확보한 바 있으며, 트럼프는 이 지분 가치가 600억 달러를 넘는다고 주장했다. Micron (MU): 6/24 회계연도 3분기 실적 발표를 앞두고 상승. 로젠블랫이 목표가를 $600→$1,200으로 상향. Marvell (MRVL): 장중 +7~12% 급등(6/22 S&P500 편입 예정). Lam Research(LRCX)·KLA(KLAC)·Applied Materials(AMAT): 칩 랠리에 동반 상승(프리마켓 +4~5%대). 단, 6/18 정확한 확정 종가는 미확인. AI 옵틱스/네트워킹: Credo(CRDO) +5%, Coherent(COHR) +4%, Applied Digital +2%대. 코퍼→광케이블 전환 수혜로 AI·데이터센터 인프라 자금 유입 지속. Lumentum(LITE)도 강세 흐름(연초 대비 +160%대의 대표 강세주). 빅테크 Apple (AAPL): 약 +0.5~1.5% — 인텔 협력 뉴스로 강보합. 마이크로소프트·구글·아마존·메타·테슬라: 나스닥 강세에 동반 상승한 것으로 추정되나 개별 6/18 확정 등락률은 본 리서치에서 확보하지 못함(아래 Caveats 참조). 네오클라우드/전력 CoreWeave (CRWV): 약 +3.3% (약 $119) — 6/22 나스닥100 편입 예정. 같은 날(6/18) $12.5억 달러 USD채(9.625%, 2032년 만기)와 €20억 유로채(8.5%) 발행을 완료했다. 1분기 매출 +127%($20.8억), 수주잔고 약 994억 달러. Nebius (NBIS): $286.69, +2.06% — 6/22 나스닥100 편입 예정. 연초 대비 +165%, 시가총액 약 564억 달러. 메타와 270억 달러 규모 계약 체결 이력.  Bloom Energy (BE): 약 +8.6% (장중, 약 $309) — AI 데이터센터 온사이트 전력 수요 테마. 1분기 매출 +130%($7.51억), 흑자 전환.  Vistra (VST): AI 전력 테마 대표주(가스·원자력 발전)이나 6/18 확정 데이터는 미확인. 매크로 이벤트 연준 (FOMC, 6/17 결정 → 6/18 시장 소화)  기준금리 3.50~3.75% 동결(4회 연속), 만장일치 12-0 가결(4월의 8-4 분열에서 큰 변화). 케빈 워시(Kevin Warsh) 신임 의장의 첫 회의. 점도표가 매파로 전환: 18명 중 9명이 2026년 최소 1회 인상을 지지했고, 이 중 6명은 0.25%p씩 두 차례(0.5%p) 인상을 지지했다. 8명은 동결, 1명은 인하 전망. 연말 금리 중앙값은 3.8%로 3월(3.4%, 인하 1회 전망)에서 반전됐다. 워시 본인은 점도표를 제출하지 않았다. 인플레 전망 대폭 상향: 2026년 헤드라인 PCE 3.6%(3월 2.7%), 코어 3.3%. GDP는 2.2%로 하향(3월 2.4%). 성명서를 130단어로 축소하고 완화 편향 문구를 삭제. CME 페드워치 기준 트레이더들은 10월 인상 확률을 60.7%로 반영. 6/17 시장 충격: 다우 -507p(-0.98%), S&P -1.21%, 2년물 국채금리가 16bp 급등한 4.21%로 1년여 최고치, 달러지수는 약 +1%로 1년래 최대폭 상승. S&P500은 "신임 의장 취임 후 최악의 Fed day"(1994년 이후)를 기록했다.  금리·달러 (6/18)  10년물 국채금리: 4.44% (전일 4.46%에서 하락), 30년물 4.86%(3개월 최저). 2년물: 4.18~4.20% (2026년 고점 부근). 단기물에는 매파 압력이 지속되는 반면 장기물은 지정학 완화·유가 하락으로 안정. 달러인덱스: 약 100.32, -0.03% (전일 100.72 연중 고점에서 하락, 리스크온 영향).  경제지표 (6/18 발표)  주간 신규 실업수당 청구: 22.6만건 (전주 23.0만→4천건 감소, 예상 22.5만 부합). 계속청구 181만건(약 3개월 최고). 저해고·저고용 기조 지속. 5월 소매판매 발표(4월은 전월비 +0.5%). 美 휘발유 평균가가 갤런당 $3.999로 3/30 이후 처음 $4 아래로 하락, 28일 연속 하락(2023년 11월 이후 최장 연속 하락).  지정학 — 미–이란 분쟁 (Operation Epic Fury)  2026년 2월 28일 美·이스라엘의 대이란 공습(美 코드명 Operation Epic Fury, 이스라엘 Operation Roaring Lion)으로 전쟁 발발. 개전 초기 이란 최고지도자 하메네이가 사망했다. 3월 4일 이란이 호르무즈 해협 봉쇄를 선언, IEA 추산 중동 산유량 1,400만bpd 이상이 차단된 사상 최대 규모의 원유 공급 차질이 발생했다. 6/17: 트럼프와 페제시키안 이란 대통령이 14개항 MOU에 원격 서명(트럼프는 G7 정상회의 후 베르사유궁 만찬 중 서명). 60일 내 최종 합의 목표로, 호르무즈 재개방·美 해상봉쇄 해제·이란 핵 프로그램 제한·"모든 종류"의 제재 해제·3,000억 달러 재건 패키지를 포함. 6/18: 美 중부사령부(CENTCOM)가 "대통령 지시에 따라 이란 항구·연안 봉쇄를 해제했다"고 공식 발표. 사우디 깃발 초대형 유조선 3척(원유 600만 배럴)이 봉쇄 해제 직후 호르무즈를 통과. 다만 이란의 완전 재개방 착수 여부는 불확실하며, 레바논 남부에서 이스라엘 공습으로 사상자가 발생하는 등 잔존 리스크는 남아 있다. 시장 영향: WTI ~$75.83(-1.25%, 3월 초 이후 최저), 브렌트 ~$78.41(-1.4%). 유가는 5일간 약 14% 하락(단, 개전 전 대비로는 여전히 30%대 높은 수준). IEA는 월간 보고서에서 2027년 공급이 수요를 약 505만bpd 초과(공급 +800만bpd→1억1,000만bpd vs 수요 +200만bpd→1억530만bpd)하는 구조적 공급 과잉을 경고했다.  그날의 핵심 테마와 투자심리  AI 반도체 슈퍼사이클 재점화: 인텔–애플 딜이 직접적 촉매가 되어 SOX가 하루 +6.67% 급등. 6/4~6/5 칩주 급락("$1조 시총 증발")과 6/17 FOMC 충격을 딛고 시장 주도권이 다시 반도체로 회귀했다. 지정학 리스크 완화 → 섹터 로테이션: 미–이란 평화 합의로 유가·에너지·방산은 약세, 반면 시클리컬·소형주는 강세(러셀2000 +2.12%로 주도). 전형적인 리스크온 로테이션. 매파 Fed vs 리스크온의 줄다리기: 단기금리에 인상 압력이 남았음에도, 장기금리 하락 + 유가·지정학 완화가 주가를 끌어올렸다. "고금리 장기화(higher-for-longer)"가 긴축 재개가 아닌 한 밸류에이션에 우호적이라는 해석이 우세. 나스닥100·S&P500 리밸런싱(6/22 발효): 200개 이상 추종상품·약 8,000억 달러 운용자산이 영향받는 가운데 CoreWeave(시총 약 522억 달러)·Nebius·Astera Labs(직전 1개월 +80%, 연초 대비 +121%)·Rocket Lab·Teradyne가 나스닥100에 편입(Charter, Zscaler, Cognizant, Insmed, Verisk 제외). AI 인프라·우주 비중 확대가 뚜렷. Marvell은 S&P500에 편입.  다음 이벤트: 다음 주 마이크론(6/24)·페덱스 실적, 6월 말 1분기 GDP 확정치와 5월 PCE 물가.  Caveats — 유의사항  개별 확정 종가 미확인: LRCX·KLAC·AMAT·LMT·VST의 6/18 정확한 마감 등락률은 본 리서치에서 확정하지 못했다(칩주는 프리마켓·장중 상승 방향, 방산주 LMT는 하락 방향까지만 확인). VIX: 약 16.4(약 -11%)는 장중 후반~종가 근사치이며, VIX가 오후 4시15분까지 거래되는 특성상 공식 settlement와 미세한 차이가 있을 수 있다. 빅테크: MSFT·GOOGL·AMZN·META·TSLA의 6/18 개별 확정 등락률은 확보하지 못했다(나스닥 +1.91% 강세에 동반 상승한 것으로 추정). 데이터 출처 혼용 주의: 일부 매체가 6/17과 6/18 데이터를 혼용하므로 날짜 확인이 필요하다. 지수 종가는 TheStreet·CNBC·Trading Economics, 개별 확정 종가는 StockAnalysis(4:00 PM EDT 마감 기준), SOX는 나스닥 공식 지수 데이터, 금리·유가는 Trading Economics·RFE/RL·Edward Jones, FOMC는 연준·CNBC·PBS를 기준으로 정리했다.  본 자료는 정보제공용으로 투자조언이 아닙니다

2/The forum then shifted focus to the power of personal connection. The transformative impact of one-to-one friendship programs between refugees and local volunteers was shared, with organizations currently facilitating hundreds of active matches. A central message was that such friendships are not a one-sided form of charity but a mutual process of learning and enrichment that actively builds social cohesion from the ground up.  The experiences of specific communities were brought to life through personal narratives. Representatives from a Ukrainian community organization detailed how they have provided leadership training, employment support, and mental health services to hundreds of new arrivals since 2022. A personal story from a Ukrainian mother who arrived with her son, navigating immense uncertainty, illustrated the journey from vulnerability to empowerment, as she now works to support other women in similar situations.   Another narrative shared how art became a universal language for coping with loneliness and mental health challenges after being forced to leave Ukraine. This led to the founding of a creative community hub inspired by the interconnected structure of a honeycomb, which plans to use art to explore themes of anxiety, displacement, and decision-making, demonstrating its power to unite people across cultures.

fullfact.org/crime/grooming-… This @FullFact piece is a textbook exercise in institutional damage control—meticulously debunking "unreliable" high-end extrapolations while burying the uncomfortable ethnic patterns and the reasons for the data voids that make precise counts impossible. It correctly notes that Sarah Champion's ~1 million figure was a rough extrapolation from Rotherham's 1,400 known victims (itself a conservative Jay Report estimate for 1997–2013), scaled nationally with reporting assumptions, and that she later called it unreliable. Lord Pearson's 250,000 likewise rests on similar national scaling from Rotherham, Telford, and Oxford. Fair enough on methodological limits: single-town extrapolations assume uniform rates, which may not hold perfectly.  But the shredding comes in what it omits and how it frames the uncertainty. The article leans heavily on "we cannot know the scale" and flaws in data sets, echoing the very institutional failures the Casey audit (2025) later exposed: ethnicity "shied away from" and unrecorded in two-thirds of cases, not due to neutral incompetence but active avoidance "for fear of appearing racist" or raising "community tensions." Local data from Greater Manchester (54% Asian suspects vs. ~double population share), West/South Yorkshire shows clear disproportionality among Pakistani-heritage men in group-based exploitation. High-profile inquiries repeatedly documented the same asymmetric predation—cohesive networks exploiting out-group (White British) vulnerability. Even government taskforces have identified and protected over 4,000 victims in single 12-month periods amid broader CSE flags around 17,000 contact offenses annually—figures that underscore how official undercounts (e.g., ~700 recorded group-based offenses in 2023 per COCAD) are grotesque minima.  The piece treats the "paucity of data" as a neutral barrier to high estimates, but this paucity is the scandal: decades of suppressed recording, downgraded charges, victim-blaming, and elite universalism that pathologizes noticing group differences in cohesion, cultural norms, and ethnocentric strategies. General child sexual abuse surveys (~7.5% before age 16) provide broad baselines but do not isolate organized grooming by culturally distant, high-solidarity groups. By focusing on debunking specific numbers without pressing the ethnic drivers or the costs of suppression, the article functions as gaslighting—downplaying a foreseeable outcome of demographic shifts and maladaptive policies that prioritize 'relations' over native daughters' safety. The true scale remains obscured by design, demanding precision from corrupted data while ignoring the patterns is how the cover persists.

They don’t know how repairs and maintenance will be done… Is anyone thinking? 🤔   Very important—often critical— Why On-Site 24/7 Hands-On Staff Matters  Data centers run 24/7 with complex, interdependent systems (power, cooling, networking, servers). Automation, DCIM (Data Center Infrastructure Management), AI-driven monitoring, and remote operations centers handle routine tasks and early detection well, but they have limits: •Rapid response to cascading or abnormal incidents: Automated systems can correct single faults, but issues often cascade (e.g., power sags tripping multiple chillers). On-site staff can physically intervene—resetting equipment, isolating problems, or performing manual restarts—before they cause outages.1 •Incident prevention and “saves”: Uptime Institute data shows staff performing maintenance and detecting issues on-site account for ~42% of avoided incidents (more than equipment redundancy at 38%). Proper staffing helps facilities run as designed for reliability and efficiency.0 •Human error is still a top outage cause (often 2/3 to 4/5 of major incidents involve it, via procedure non-compliance, misconfigurations, or operational issues), but well-trained on-site teams with good processes also prevent many problems. Understaffing exacerbates risks.21 Uptime Institute recommendation: For Tier III or IV facilities (high criticality, concurrently maintainable or fault-tolerant), have at least 1–2 qualified operators on site 24/7. This is a prescriptive standard for Operational Sustainability certifications due to quick response needs.03 Real-World Examples of Staffing Impact •Microsoft Azure outage in Sydney (2023): A utility power sag tripped cooling chillers. Some auto-restarted, but 13 needed manual intervention. Only three people were on site at night, delaying recovery and causing extended downtime (services affected for hours). Microsoft temporarily increased night staffing and improved automation.17 •Distributed or edge sites without on-site staff often see longer downtime because issues go unnoticed or require truck rolls (costly and slow).5 Trends: Automation Reduces But Doesn’t Eliminate the Need •Hyperscale/ large facilities (100+ MW): Staffing ratios have dropped to ~0.2–0.3 people per MW thanks to AI predictive maintenance, robotics, and remote NOC (Network Operations Centers). Routine monitoring and some tasks are automated.25 •Smaller or high-density sites (e.g., AI workloads with high heat): Higher ratios (2–3 per MW) and more hands-on needs for hardware swaps, cabling, or complex cooling.25 •Hybrid models common: Remote monitoring + on-site “Remote Hands/Smart Hands” (technicians for physical tasks) or on-call escalation. Colocation providers often supply 24/7 on-site support as a service.8 •AI/automation is a workforce multiplier (reducing some roles, upskilling others), but physical intervention, security, compliance, and complex troubleshooting still require people. Talent shortages make retaining skilled on-site staff challenging.26 Risks of minimal/no on-site staff: •Delayed incident response → longer outages (downtime costs can be enormous). •Over-reliance on automation that fails or needs human override. •Burnout on thin teams and compliance/SLA gaps. When it’s less critical: Low-tier, non-mission-critical, or highly standardized edge sites can lean more on remote + occasional on-site visits, but this increases vulnerability. Bottom Line For reliable operations in critical environments, on-site 24/7 hands-on availability (even if lean, with strong training and escalation paths) is a high-importance factor in uptime, risk mitigation, and preventing minor issues from becoming major outages. It’s not just about headcount—it’s about qualified people who know the site, combined with automation. Many operators balance this with outsourced Remote Hands for flexibility while maintaining core on-site coverage.  My thought: So does AI Up-Time matter?💩

⚠️ New iPhone BootROM Vulnerability Exposes Apple SoCs to Full Chain-of-Trust Compromise  Source: cybersecuritynews.com/iphone…  A novel BootROM vulnerability, dubbed usbliter8, affects Apple devices powered by A12, S4/S5, and A13 SoCs. The exploit chains a hardware-level bug in the Synopsys DWC2 USB controller with a firmware configuration flaw, enabling full application processor boot-chain compromise with no software patch possible due to the immutable nature of BootROM code.  The vulnerability originates in how the DWC2 USB controller handles consecutive USB Setup packets. The controller stores up to three Setup packets in memory before resetting the DMA base address (stored in the DOEPDMA register) to its starting position, functioning like a ring buffer.  #cybersecuritynews

Kevin Hand, Alien Oceans. One of the most relevant books for the project. Hand directly connects Earth’s oceans with extraterrestrial ocean worlds. NASA Roadmap to Ocean Worlds. Necessary for Europa, Enceladus, Titan, Triton, and the scientific programme of ocean-world exploration. F. Minerals, Energy, Metallurgy Vaclav Smil, Energy and Civilization. Essential anti-romantic foundation. Smil reminds the essay that civilization is constrained by energy, materials, scale, and thermodynamic reality. Vaclav Smil, How the World Really Works. Useful for steel, concrete, ammonia, plastics, energy, food, and the material dependence of modern civilization. IEA, Global Critical Minerals Outlook. Empirical grounding for lithium, cobalt, nickel, copper, graphite, rare earths, refining concentration, demand growth, and strategic vulnerability. International Seabed Authority materials. Necessary for deep-sea minerals, polymetallic nodules, cobalt-rich crusts, sulphides, and the governance problem of seabed extraction. G. Failure, Procedure, Institutional Memory Charles Perrow, Normal Accidents. Essential for complex, tightly coupled systems. Perrow explains why submarines, spacecraft, nuclear systems, cable networks, and habitats require failure anticipation.

Exceptional rigor. These cutting-edge works peel back narrative fluff to map the cold algebra of authoritarian markets and state vulnerability. My deepest respect to this new generation of US scholars—pure institutional logic, zero paralysis

11/16  This does not mean that private enterprise is irrelevant. Private firms can accelerate, innovate, reduce costs, and break bureaucratic stagnation. But the deeper architecture of abyssal and cosmic competence cannot be reduced to entrepreneurship. No individual company creates metallurgy, maritime law, orbital regimes, universities, naval traditions, geodetic systems, rescue norms, strategic infrastructure, or the accumulated scientific culture on which it depends. The entrepreneur stands on a buried state, a buried academy, a buried military, a buried infrastructure, and a buried history of public risk. Space rhetoric often prefers the heroic private figure because he is narratively simple. The state is slow, the founder is vivid. The institution is tedious, the visionary quotable. But abyssal civilization cannot be governed by quotation. It requires continuity after the speech: repair schedules, insurance, ports, standards, courts, training, export controls, environmental law, safety boards, and the ability to say no to profitable stupidity. The same is true of the ocean. The surface image of maritime power is the ship. The deeper reality is logistics, maintenance, charts, ports, weather knowledge, repair yards, crews, law, insurance, fuel, cables, surveillance, anti-submarine warfare, and institutional memory. Sea power is not merely the possession of vessels. It is the organized capacity to sustain movement, communication, denial, protection, and repair across an environment that belongs fully to no one. Orbital power will have a similar structure. Satellites, stations, lunar assets, relays, fuel depots, habitats, robotic systems, and planetary missions will require not only launch capacity but jurisdiction, traffic management, debris mitigation, rescue norms, military restraint, industrial supply chains, and dispute mechanisms. Space without institutions becomes not freedom but danger at altitude. A civilization becomes abyssal when it learns that depth cannot be improvised. It becomes spacefaring when it transfers that lesson beyond Earth. XIII. Command or Plunder: The Moral Test of the Abyss The word command is dangerous because it can become a euphemism for domination. To command the depths must not mean to seize, exhaust, poison, militarize, and abandon them. That would not be abyssal competence. It would be surface barbarism extended downward. The distinction is exact. Knowledge is not plunder. Operation is not plunder. Protection is not plunder. Repair is not plunder. Governance is not plunder. But extraction without restraint, secrecy without accountability, militarization without law, and technological access without ecological intelligence are plunder. A civilization that treats every newly accessible environment as a resource frontier has not matured. It has merely improved the reach of its appetites. The ocean already reveals this danger. It is subject to overfishing, pollution, dumping, noise, seabed disturbance, cable vulnerability, naval competition, legal ambiguity, and the growing temptation of deep-sea mining. The abyss is increasingly entered not only by science but by capital, war, and strategic anxiety. The fact that man can reach deeper does not prove that he has become deeper. The moral issue is not external to the technical one. Plunder is bad procedure at civilizational scale. It destroys the conditions of knowledge, ignores delayed consequences, treats warning signs as obstacles, and converts inaccessible environments into profit before they have been understood. The civilization that rips up the seabed without knowing what it is doing is not decisive. It is epistemically primitive. It mistakes access for comprehension. This matters for space because the habits developed on Earth will not be left behind. A civilization that cannot govern its oceans will carry the same disorder into orbit, onto the Moon, to Mars, and eventually toward ocean worlds.

sample_tweets.text: x.com/i/article/206774267338…

2026년 AI와 대한민국의 현주소 <박태웅 녹서포럼 의장>  1. 글로벌 AI 인프라의 폭발과 한국의 독보적 수혜 AI 공급망의 병목 이동 초기 AI 붐에서는 데이터와 GPU가 병목이었으나, 현재는 메모리 반도체(HBM)와 에너지(전력 인프라)가 거대한 병목으로 떠올랐습니다.  막대한 미래 매출(백로그) 빅테크 기업들이 반도체 공급 부족으로 계약하고도 매출로 잡지 못한 금액(백로그)이 폭증하고 있습니다. 구글 클라우드는 4,600억 달러(약 600조 원), 마이크로소프트는 6,270억 달러(약 945조 원)에 달합니다.   한국의 '대박' 국면 서방 진영의 강력한 제조창 역할을 하는 한국은 고부가 가치 메모리(HBM4), 초고압 변압기, 전선, 원자력 발전, 방산, 조선 등 전 분야에서 수혜를 보고 있습니다. 삼성전자와 SK하이닉스의 합산 영업이익이 일본 상장사 상위 100개 기업의 총합을 압도할 정도입니다.   2. 에지 컴퓨터와 HBM4의 진화 (삼성전자의 기회) 메모리 수요의 폭증 젠슨 황(엔비디아 CEO)이 선언한 '에지(Agent) 시대'에는 AI가 사람 대신 지속적으로 자율 연산 및 저장을 해야 하므로 기존보다 메모리 수요가 30배~100배 이상 급증합니다.   HBM4와 시스템 반도체의 결합 HBM4부터는 메모리에 연산 기능(로직)을 합치게 됩니다. 이는 메모리 기술과 파운드리(위탁생산) 능력을 동시에 모두 갖춘 삼성전자에게 기술적 주도권을 되찾아올 수 있는 거대한 기회가 될 수 있습니다.  3. 현실로 다가온 지능 노동의 대체 AI는 거품이 아니라 이미 실전에 완벽히 정착했습니다. 우크라이나 전장에서는 팔란티어 AI가 작전 속도를 100배 이상 끌어올렸고, 소프트웨어 코딩 시장은 엔트로픽(클로드)과 구글이 이미 자사 코드의 90~100%를 AI로 짜고 있습니다.  그 외에도 보안(취약점 진단), 법률, 회계 등 인간 고유의 영역이라 여겨졌던 '인지 노동'이 한 칸씩 빠르게 대체되고 있습니다.   4. AI 주권(소버린 AI)과 한국의 협상력 안보, 국방 데이터 및 개인 의료 데이터 보호를 위해서 자체 AI 파운데이션 모델을 확보하는 'AI 주권'은 선택이 아닌 필수입니다.   한국은 독자 포털(네이버 등)을 운영하며 대용량 데이터를 처리해 본 인프라와 개발자가 풍부해, 글로벌 '주목할 만한 AI 모델' 8개를 보유한 세계 3위 국가입니다. 핵심 공급망(반도체, 전력 등)을 쥐고 있으므로 글로벌 AI 윤리·안전 표준 제정에서도 강력한 발언권과 협상력을 행사할 수 있습니다.   5. 청년 일자리 위기와 'AI 기본 사회'로의 전환 제안 부분 최적화의 비극 기업들이 비용 절감을 위해 신입(조교, 수습 등) 채용을 줄이고 AI로 대체하면서 청년 일자리가 직격탄을 맞고 있습니다. 이를 방치하면 10년 뒤 사회의 허리(숙련공)가 사라지는 파국을 맞이하므로 국가가 나서야 합니다.  노동 시간 단축의 필연성 과거 주 6일에서 주 5일로 단축했을 때 오히려 한계 기업이 정리되고 우량 기업으로 자원이 이동해 경제성장률이 올라갔듯, 생산성 향상에 맞춰 노동 시간을 줄이고 일자리를 나눠야 사회 전체의 생존이 가능합니다.  초과 세수를 통한 기본 사회 실험 AI 인프라 폭발로 국가에 막대한 추가 법인세(실탄)가 들어오고 있습니다. 이 재원을 흐지부지 쓰지 말고 신안군의 '태양광 연금(주민 참여형 이익 공유 모델)'처럼 전 국민 혹은 취약 청년층에게 'AI 기본 소득·접근권'을 보장하는 구조를 만들어야 합니다.   글로벌 리더십 선점 한국이 선제적으로 AI 자본의 독점을 막고 '모두가 상생하는 AI 기본 사회 모델'을 성공시킨다면, 현재 UN 등 국제기구가 한국에 '글로벌 AI 허브'를 만들려는 움직임과 맞물려 전 세계 가난한 남반구 국가(글로벌 사우스)를 이끄는 위대한 글로벌 리더십을 발휘할 수 있습니다.

SK Telecom이 Anthropic 수출 통제의 진짜 트리거였다?  사실이면 금번 수출통제 트리거가 우리나라였다는게 흥미롭네요.  1. SK Telecom의 Mythos 접근 권한 문제     - SK Telecom은 2023년 Anthropic에 1억 달러를 투자한 파트너사이며, Project Glasswing을 통해 Anthropic의 최상위 모델 Claude Mythos에 접근할 수 있었습니다. (Project Glasswing은 보안·신뢰도가 높은 제한된 기관·기업만 초대하는 프로그램)     - 미국 백악관(트럼프 행정부)은 SK Telecom이 과거 중국(차이나 유니콤)과 합작법인·투자 관계를 가졌다는 점을 문제 삼아, Mythos 접근 권한을 즉시 취소하라고 Anthropic에 직접 지시했습니다.     - Anthropic은 즉시 SK Telecom의 접근을 차단했습니다. (당시에는 수출 통제까지는 아니었습니다)  2. Amazon의 Fable 5 취약점 제보    - 그 직후 Amazon 연구팀이 Fable 5(Mythos의 공개용·안전장치가 강화된 버전)의 가드레일을 우회할 수 있는 취약점을 발견해 백악관에 보고했습니다.     - 이로 인해 Fable 5를 통해 Mythos 수준의 강력한 사이버·보안 능력에 접근할 위험이 제기되었습니다.  3. 결과: 광범위한 수출 통제 발동    - 백악관은 “Anthropic이 자사 최첨단 AI 기술을 안전하게 통제할 수 없다”고 판단했습니다.     - 결국 모든 외국인(미국 내 거주 외국인 포함) 에 대해 Mythos와 Fable 5 접근을 금지하는 수출 통제를 발동했습니다.     - Anthropic은 국적별로 세밀하게 차단하기가 현실적으로 어렵고 프라이버시 문제도 있어, 결국 두 모델의 접근을 전면 중단했습니다.
모두의 창업 보안 이슈 터진김에, 작은 상식 하나   CVE란 말이 들어봤을겁니다. Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures 약자로 공개적으로 알려진 보안 취약점 목록을 표준화한 국제 식별 체계입니다.  보안 전문가 및 기업이 특정 취약점에 대하여 정확하게 소통하고 대응할 수 있도록 돕는데 사용합니다.
