2/2  The US has nothing to show for its efforts, and at considerable cost.  Thousands of innocent civilians have been killed, millions more have been displaced, and hundreds of children are dead after a US missile struck a school in Minab.  Trump and his cabinet are complicit in war crimes.  We've also lost US troops and Iranian missiles have damaged several key US military bases in the middle east.  Worse yet, the Islamic Regime is now more entrenched than it was prior to the war and is emerging as a more brutal, militant dictatorship.  The IRGC is still a powerful authority in the region and has deeper ties to the new hardline Ayatollah.  The people of Iran will continue to suffer at the hands of a more punishing and violent regime.  We have failed to "liberate" the Iranian people from the clutches of their repressive government.   This war has wreaked a devastating toll on the world, on the American economy and taxpayer, on our alliances and global reputation, on our munitions stockpile, on the Iranian and Lebanese people, on our Persian Gulf allies, on our very credibility, on western democracy itself.  And for what!?  Nothing.  No, LESS than nothing.  This so-called "memorandum of understanding" is just playing for time.  And after all of the damage and suffering that Trump's war has caused, there's no reality where Iran does not come out on top.  The regime has only GAINED leverage in future negotiations and has risen from the conflict a more formidable adversary.   Trump's actions have one-sidedly benefitted the enemy, China, Russia, the IRGC...  After all of the chaos that Trump's war has unleashed on the world, Iran will have secured greater ownership over its nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles program.  They will still remain a regional sponsor of terrorism, they will still control the waterways in and around the Strait.  They will continue to extort the US government and will receive sanction relief and/or some form of war reparations so that, in return, Donald Trump can claim he brought "peace" to the region and can finally wash his hands of the war.  But Donald Trump is no peacemaker. He's an incompetent and belligerent war criminal with an appetite for conquest that's being fueled entirely by his ego.  His mishandling of the war has come at a considerable cost to the American people.  More than that, his actions have reignited old conflicts and have plunged the world into disarray.  Trump and his cabinet have repeatedly LIED about the pretext for this war; insisting that Iran was building a nuclear weapon.  Netanyahu has been pushing the same warnings for decades.  But our own intelligence community says otherwise. Iran was NOT building a nuclear weapon and posed no imminent threat to our country.  DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified before Congress:  > “The Intelligence Community continues to assess that Iran is not building a nuclear weapon and that Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has not authorized the nuclear weapons program he suspended in 2003.”  A top US counterterrorism official also resigned, stating, "Iran never posed an imminent threat."  Trump lied about operation "midnight hammer," and he continues to lie about the looming risk of an Iranian nuclear weapon.  Obama's deal intended to avoid a war and box in Iran's nuclear program.  By all accounts, Iran was committed to its end of the bargain before Trump abandoned the previous nuclear deal OUT OF SPITE for his predecessor.  This was according to US intelligence, the International Atomic Energy Agency, and even Trump administration officials at the time.  Iran only began taking steps towards enriching uranium after Trump withdrew from the JCPOA. Their nuclear program became more advanced than it would have been under Obama's deal.  And now Trump will drag these negotiations out for as long as he can until he's forced to accept a final agreement that will be far more conciliatory towards Iran than the deal Obama negotiated through DIPLOMACY, not WAR!

🇨🇦 Canada - Canada Life  A threat actor has advertised an alleged database associated with Canada Life containing more than 5.5 million records on a cybercrime forum.  Based on the published sample, the dataset appears to contain customer, employee, and platform-related information, including user profiles, contact details, organizational data, permissions, and account management fields.  Alleged exposed data includes:  * 5.5+ million records * Names * Email addresses * Company information * Department and title information * Address-related data * Country and location fields * User account metadata * Employee identifiers * Manager and approver information * User permissions and access controls * Communication preferences and notification settings  Organization details:  * Organization: Canada Life * Country: Canada * Industry: Insurance & Financial Services * Status: Alleged database leak  The authenticity of the dataset and the method of acquisition have not been independently verified. The published sample suggests the data may originate from an internal business application, CRM platform, or employee/customer management system.  Potential risks include:  * Targeted phishing attacks * Business email compromise (BEC) * Employee impersonation * Identity theft * Social engineering campaigns * Credential-based attacks against corporate systems  Analyst Note: Financial and insurance organizations remain high-value targets due to the large volume of personal, employment, and financial information they maintain. Even partial exposure of internal organizational structures can significantly enhance social engineering and fraud operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Canada

If the issue is U.S. aid, settlements, or military actions, those should be named directly. Without specifics, it sounds more like frustration than analysis.  The point about Donald #Trump is partly valid—his administration did shift the regional landscape—but claiming he removed Israel’s “existential threat” is an “Alice in wonderland statement”👈. Key tensions, especially involving Iran 🇮🇷, didn’t disappear they are still there‼️😡  The “not our existential threat” argument reflects a more isolationist view of U.S. policy, which is fair—but it overlooks strategic ties like intelligence sharing and regional influence.  Bottom line: your argument is there but has no real basis, 👉it relies on absolutes 👈and lacks 👉evidence. 👈specifics are needed and please 🤔👉drop the sweeping claims, because when that happens it may 🤔👉become more convincing.👈

18 Juin 2026   Des pirates informatiques russes ont mené l'opération Broken Byte, qui leur a permis d'accéder à plus de 50 000 caméras de surveillance en Europe de l'Est et en Ukraine.  D'après la publication, le groupe NoName057(16) et le pirate informatique PalachPro sont à l'origine de l'attaque. La cyber-attaque devrait durer environ deux semaines, selon la source du portail.  « Nous allons tout paralyser :  les banques, le complexe militaro-industriel, les infrastructures critiques, et nous allons également réserver quelques surprises à la logistique et au transport ferroviaire dans quelques pays », a déclaré le pirate informatique, selon la source.  l'opération Broken Byte a débuté le 15 juin, en marge du sommet du G7 en France.  Des pirates informatiques sont parvenus à injecter un logiciel dans les caméras afin de scanner les adresses IP, d'identifier les vulnérabilités des systèmes et d'accéder à l'infrastructure.  Le pirate informatique PalachPro a affirmé avoir piraté plus de 6 000 caméras de vidéosurveillance dans des bâtiments administratifs, militaires et municipaux à travers l'Ukraine.  SecPost a réussi à le contacter. PalachPro a indiqué que le piratage avait été effectué à l'aide d'un scanner réseau propriétaire et que son but était d'obtenir des données sur la localisation des systèmes de défense aérienne et l'activité des forces armées ukrainiennes, données qui seraient ensuite transmises aux forces armées russes.   L'intelligence artificielle est également utilisée pour capturer diverses données :  visages, plaques d'immatriculation, couleur des véhicules, vêtements, etc.  Les informations collectées sont enregistrées dans des fichiers géoréférencés et chargées dans une base de données.  De ce fait, des pirates informatiques ont accédé à plus de 50 000 caméras en Europe de l'Est et en Ukraine. Cela leur permet de suivre les déplacements de marchandises ou de personnes. Les pirates informatiques eux-mêmes admettent que la cyberattaque pourrait causer des dommages importants aux infrastructures des pays de l'UE.  « Le traitement et la transmission des données seront considérablement perturbés, ce qui entraînera des ralentissements aux points de contrôle, aux feux de circulation, aux systèmes d'éclairage public, aux panneaux d'information sur les transports et aux installations industrielles », décrit l'article, évoquant les conséquences probables.  Les dommages préliminaires causés par la cyber-attaque sont estimés à plus d'un million de dollars.  En avril, les médias allemands ont rapporté que les services de renseignement allemands enquêtaient sur une vaste campagne d'hameçonnage visant les utilisateurs de la messagerie Signal.  L'implication de Moscou est envisagée...Bien évidement 😅

How can machine learning optimise industrial processes, saving your business time and money?  Industrial businesses generate huge volumes of data — vibration, current, temperature, pressure, images, process logs and more. The challenge is often not collecting data, but turning it into better decisions.  Fault Detection & Predictive Maintenance Predict machine failures before they happen using variables such as vibration, current, temperature, noise and pressure. Machine learning can identify complex patterns associated with degradation and estimate failure risk — helping reduce catastrophic downtime and unnecessary maintenance.  Anomaly Detection Detect unusual operating conditions automatically from historical data. Unlike traditional rule-based systems, anomaly detection can identify unexpected behaviour even when examples of previous failures are unavailable.  Sensor Fusion & Calibration Many industrial systems rely on multiple sensors with different viewpoints, offsets, noise characteristics and nonlinear distortions. Machine learning can combine and calibrate these signals to produce more accurate and reliable measurements and conclusions.  Yield & Process Optimisation Want to maximise throughput, yield or product quality? Machine learning can learn the relationship between process inputs and outcomes, helping identify operating conditions that improve performance and reduce waste.  Examples include: • Manufacturing • Energy systems • Medical devices • Scientific instrumentation • Robotics and automation  Our PhD consultants develop advanced machine learning, optimisation and sensor data processing algorithms to solve real industrial problems.  Follow our page, or learn more by visiting our website:  mathematicsconsultants.com/2…

Dude, where are my tokens?  Plenty of tools tell you how many tokens Claude Code and Codex burned — ccusage and friends do that well.  Leet Agent Tracker answers the harder question: what was the agent actually doing when it spent them?   It's a free, open-source, local-first agent-trace data warehouse that pulls in the session logs already on your machine — across all your projects, different harnesses (currently Claude Code + Codex), and support multiple accounts per harness — and unifies them into one immutable vault. Then it rebuilds the full story of every run as a readable, attributed transcript: every human prompt, the chain of work it triggered, each tool call and LLM round-trip. Everything runs locally, your data never leaves your computer.  One command to start the web UI: $ npx @leettools/leet-agent-tracker -y  The first time run will pull the dependency on DuckDB and start the ingestion automatically.  What makes it different:  🌐 One view across everything — projects, harnesses, and providers aggregated together, so you stop stitching usage from separate tools. 🧾 Align usage with your subscription — point it at any subscription/billing window and it reconstructs the exact prompts and LLM calls behind that spend, with saved snapshots. When your plan resets or you change tiers, you can line your real activity up against it and see what each window actually paid for. 🔁 Prompt sequences — one row per human prompt, expanded into the entire iteration it triggered: every follow-up turn, sub-agent, tool call, and retry. 📄 Full attributed session transcripts — replay any session end to end and export it to markdown (identical from the UI or CLI), so the analytics always tie back to the real conversation.  ==== Want the transcript without opening the UI? One command dumps a project's prompt sequences as markdown (after the logs have been ingested, use --format markdown if you want to have the full transcripts as a markdown file):  $ npx @leettools/leet-agent-tracker project sequences <project>  GitHub: github.com/pengfeng/leet-age… · MIT licensed — would love your feedback!

Oxford debate is done! Kudos to the amazing team of @emilykschrader @HenMazzig @AviMayer  More impressions later, but for now - here is the text of my talk in opposition to the proposition "This House Believes Israel Never Truly Wanted Peace with Palestine":  Israel wanted peace. Truly. Repeatedly.  And yet, from the minute the United Nations voted for the Partition Plan in 1947, we have been under attack. The Arabs threatened to use force to prevent the establishment of a Jewish state on any part of our homeland, and they proceeded to make good on that threat.  And what did that Partition Plan call for anyway? Was it some grave injustice to the Arabs? No. Most of the fertile land was to go to what would have become the Palestinian Arab state, while the Jewish state would be relegated to three pieces of barely contiguous, mostly arid non-arable territory.  And yet, the Jews said yes. But the Arabs said no. First the Palestinian Arab militias attacked and then in May 1948, every Arab nation surrounding us and beyond invaded. And this is the only reason why there were ever any refugees from that war.  The only reason.  Israel managed to survive the 1948 Arab war, and we would have been happy to just live in peace.  Remember, at the end of the 1948 war, Jordan annexed the West Bank and Egypt administered Gaza. There was nothing – absolutely nothing - to prevent them from creating one more Arab state on these territories for the Palestinians. They did not.  Instead, they chose to continue the war against Israel, which is how Israel came to control the West Bank and Gaza. It was not in some aggressive land grab. It was after multiple Arab armies surrounded us - again - and in May 1967, announced: “This is our chance Arabs, to deal Israel a mortal blow of annihilation . . .”  Israel won that war in six days, but even then, we offered peace, making it, as Abba Eban (of Cambridge, much better I believe, I went there too…) wryly observed “the first war in history that on the morrow the victors sued for peace and the vanquished called for unconditional surrender."  And, what was the Arab and Palestinian response to Israel offering peace? "No to peace with Israel, no to recognition of Israel, no to negotiations".  But we still hoped for peace. So, we entered the Oslo Peace process in 1993. And in September 2000, just when many Israelis, including me, truly believed that a two-state solution was close, Palestinians launched the Second Intifada escalating into a brutal campaign of suicide bombings that left over 1000 Israelis shred to bloody pieces with thousands more maimed for life.  Yet, through all that, when in December 2000, President Bill Clinton put a peace deal on the table that would have given the Palestinians all of Gaza, 96% of the West Bank with comparable land swaps from within Israel, no settlements, safe passage between the two territories, most of the Old City, Arab East Jerusalem for a capital and an international force replacing the IDF on the West Bank - it was Israel that said yes.  There was no excuse for the Palestinians not to take this deal. None. But the Palestinians, led by Yasser Arafat, walked away, choosing instead to continue blowing us up through suicide bombings.  Ultimately this led to the construction of the security barrier, checkpoints, roadblocks in the West Bank which thankfully brought, for a while, an end to this reign of bloody terror.  You would think we would get the message, but in 2008 it was Israel, through Prime Minister Olmert, who made yet another peace offer, A near-total Israeli withdrawal from the West Bank, with close to 100% land swaps; No settlements, A link to the Gaza Strip; Arab East Jerusalem as capital of Palestine. Israel would have given up sovereignty over all of the Old City – including the single holiest site to the Jewish people, The Temple Mount, which would be overseen by an international trusteeship.  Again, there was no excuse for the Palestinians not to accept it. None. But under President Abbas, the Palestinians walked away from that, too.  Israel even disengaged from Gaza in 2005, removing all settlements, allowing ourselves to believe that the elected government of Hamas wanted to focus on improving their people’s lives, instead of destroying ours.  We were so desperate to believe this that we ignored all the red flags, allowing numerous Gazans to enter Israel to work, study and get specialized medical care.  But all this emphasis on economic development and work permits was part of a carefully built deception, as Hamas leaders later boasted, to lull Israelis into thinking they really were going to mind their own business and live calmly next to us – while they were planning something very very different.  On Oct 7, 2023 3000 trained and armed Palestinians invaded Israel from Gaza. Much of the intelligence they relied on came from the very Palestinians we allowed into Israel on work and study and medical permits. They massacred. They pillaged. They engaged in acts of gleeful sadistic brutality which I still lack the heart or the stomach to describe. They took 251 hostages – elderly men and women, mothers, fathers, children, babies. And it was not just trained militants, thousands of so-called “Ordinary ‘innocent’ civilian Palestinians” joined enthusiastically in the frenzy of manic violence, committing horrific acts of brutality.  And when the IDF went into Gaza, as they had to, to ensure that the Palestinians could never again do this to us, they found a completely weaponized landscape: Tunnels running under and opening into every house, mosque, hospital, and school. A land filled with booby traps making it impossible for us to fight without doing enormous damage to Gaza.   And Hamas, who remained hidden in tunnels and in civilian clothing, designed it this way. This is what we were facing.  So do not dare look at us and propose that “Israel Never Truly Wanted Peace with Palestine”. If anything, we wanted it too much. We made offer after offer of peace. We absorbed endless aggression.  But we are not going to let ourselves be annihilated to convince you that we want peace. And we will certainly not accept peace on any terms. Some of you demand a one state solution and pretend that two peoples, connected to the same land but with distinct religions, values, cultures, languages and histories, who have never lived in peace, will magically exist in peace and harmony if only they live in one state. And frankly, far too often this call for a one state solution is not naive or delusional but a carefully calculated attempt to rob Jews of their right to self-determination to be a free people in our own land.  In this, we are no fools. We have a millennia long history of being persecuted and subjugated by one people after another, and we are not about to give up our one and only state to go back to being at the mercy of others.  We do want peace and we are willing to go far to make it happen, but we will not give up our state for it.  So now, it is your turn, Palestinians, to answer the question that somehow no university seems to debate – When did you ever truly want peace with the one Jewish state?

WordPress site owners are being urged to change passwords, enable MFA, remove unknown admin accounts, and keep their sites updated, especially after police identified leaked credentials tied to 1.4 million websites.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Massive Naver Smart Store Merchant Data Leak  A darkweb forum post claims that

sample_tweets.text: 🔎 74,000 FortiGate records.  At first glance, it looked like another large credential exposure.  But ThreatMon's investigation revealed something more structured.  In our latest analysis, we examine what was exposed, what the infrastructure revealed, and why an adversary would invest resources into collecting 74,000 FortiGate-related records.  Read the full analysis to explore the findings and implications: threatmon.io/why-would-an-ad…

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Massive Naver Smart Store Merchant Data Leak  A darkweb forum post claims that a massive data breach has exposed personal and business information of Naver Smart Store merchants, allegedly including 732.000 email addresses and 1.420.00 phone numbers. According to the listing on an underground forum, the stolen data originates from sell.smartstore.naver.com, South Korea's leading e-commerce marketplace platform.  The post claims the breach includes comprehensive personally identifiable information (PII) and business details for merchants operating on the Naver Smart Store platform. The actor alleges the following data types were compromised: - First name and last name - Mobile phone numbers - Fax numbers - Email addresses - Date of birth - Physical addresses - Business registration numbers - Shopping categories - CEO names - Company addresses - Website URLs  A sample file attached to the post, named "sample_sell.smartstore.naver…_@Data1ortex_db.xlsx," displays 25 records of merchant data including company names, contact information, and business details. The data appears to represent individual sellers and small businesses operating storefronts on the platform.  Naver Smart Store is operated by Naver Corporation, one of South Korea's largest internet companies and a dominant player in the Korean digital marketplace. The platform enables individuals and businesses to create and manage online stores, serving as critical e-commerce infrastructure for hundreds of thousands of Korean merchants. If authentic, exposure of merchant PII and business registration data could enable targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and social engineering attacks against store operators.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of Naver Smart Store merchant data has not been independently verified.  #DataBreach #NaverSmartStore #ECommerceSecurity #SouthKoreaCyber #Data1ortex #PIIExposure #DarkWeb

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 8M Argentine Citizen Records on Darkweb Forum  A darkweb forum post reveals the alleged sale of a massive database containing personal information of over 8 million Argentine citizens. According to the listing, the data was scraped in May 2026 and covers individuals aged 18 to 70 across eight major provinces, including Ciudad de Buenos Aires, Catamarca, Córdoba, Corrientes, Entre Ríos, Jujuy, La Rioja, and Mendoza. The actor claims the dataset contains 8,175,364 unique rows and is being offered for 0.05 XMR per decryption key.  The post alleges that the database includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) and government-issued identifiers. The exposed data reportedly consists of: - DNI (National Identity Document) numbers - CUIT (Tax Identification Numbers) - Full names - Years of birth - Province and town of residence  To verify the legitimacy of the leak, the seller provides a sample zip file via Onionshare containing a redacted database, an encrypted vault file named vault.db, and a Python script titled restore_db.py. The actor claims that the redacted database allows buyers to cross-reference known DNI numbers, with names partially masked and birth years showing only the final digit. The accompanying Python script allegedly contains the decryption logic to restore the original database using the purchased key.  If authentic, the exposure of millions of Argentine tax and identity records could facilitate large-scale identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns across the country. Argentina's Federal Public Revenue Administration (AFIP) and national civil registry databases have historically been targets for cybercriminals seeking bulk PII.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach and the authenticity of the 8.1 million records have not been independently verified.  #DataBreach #Argentina #PII #DarkWeb #DataSale #CyberSecurity #IdentityTheft

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 3.9M Record Leak of Vietnam ISP SCTV.VN  A darkweb forum post claims the exposure of a massive database belonging to SCTV.VN, a prominent internet service provider and cable television operator in Vietnam. The listing, posted by an actor using the handle "Orcinus orca," alleges the leak contains subscriber data for 3.9 million users.  According to the post, the compromised dataset includes sensitive customer information and technical identifiers. The actor claims the following data is exposed: - Full Personally Identifiable Information (PII) - ONT (Optical Network Terminal) serial numbers  SCTV is a major telecommunications entity in Vietnam, operating as a subsidiary of the Saigon Times Corporation. The exposure of 3.9 million records represents a significant breach of consumer privacy. Additionally, the inclusion of ONT serial numbers is notable; these identifiers are used in fiber-optic networks to authenticate customer premises equipment. If authentic, the exposure of these technical artifacts could enable precise targeting of the provider's infrastructure or facilitate advanced social engineering attacks against subscribers.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of SCTV.VN has not been independently verified.  #DataLeak #SCTV #Vietnam #TelecomSecurity #DarkWeb #PII #Infrastructure

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 4.9M Wise User Records for Sale on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post alleges the sale of a database containing 4.9 million user registrations from Wise, the international money transfer service, specifically targeting customers in Spain. According to the listing, the archive purportedly includes records of individuals who have transacted on the platform's Spanish domain (wise.com/es).  The post claims the dataset contains 4.900.307 rows of highly sensitive personal and compliance data. The seller enumerates the following fields within the alleged dump: - id_usuario, timestamp_utc - perfil_personal.nombre, perfil_personal.apellidos, perfil_personal.fecha_nacimiento, perfil_personal.genero - documento_legal.tipo_doc, documento_legal.num_doc, documento_legal.pais_iso, documento_legal.estado_verificacion - canal_comunicacion.telefono.completo, canal_comunicacion.telefono.validado - canal_comunicacion.email.direccion, canal_comunicacion.email.entregable - meta_geografia.pais, meta_geografia.provincia, meta_geografia.localidad - registro_cumplimiento.estado_kyc, registro_cumplimiento.control_aml, registro_cumplimiento.terminos_wise_v4  Sample JSON records provided in the thread appear to show full names, dates of birth, verified Spanish national ID numbers (NIF/NIE), validated phone numbers, email addresses, and geographic locations. The data also allegedly includes Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) verification statuses.  Wise, formerly known as TransferWise, is a prominent financial technology company that facilitates cross-border payments and currency exchange for millions of users worldwide. If authentic, the exposure of verified identity documents and compliance records could facilitate identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns against Spanish customers.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and Wise has not issued a public statement regarding the incident.  #DataBreach #Wise #DataSale #FinanceSecurity #Spain #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 Alleged Sale of Fortinet VPN Access on a Dark Web Forum  It has been discovered that a threat actor codenamed “SantaAd” is offering Fortinet VPN credentials for sale on a popular dark web forum. The listing claims to contain approximately 34,000 sets of credentials, which are associated with 6,896 unique Fortinet VPN IP addresses, with more than 3,100 of them located in the United States. The threat actor claims that the credentials are up-to-date, that the exploited vulnerability has not yet been publicly disclosed, and that password hash dumps will also be provided to buyers.  It has been observed that the listing has been updated over time and the asking prices have been raised. In the initial posting, the starting price was set at 25,000 USD and the “Blitz” (instant purchase) price at 50,000 USD; in a subsequent update, the starting price was raised to 30,000 USD. In the most recent update, the starting price was listed as 60,000 USD, the bid increment as 25,000 USD, and the instant purchase price as 120,000 USD. Additionally, the threat actor claimed that access is still being obtained, citing recent security research affecting Fortinet firewalls. The accuracy of the claims made in the post, the nature of the mentioned security vulnerability, and the validity of the access credentials offered for sale have not been independently verified.  #Fortinet #VPN #access #sale #Darkweb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 A New Information Stealer Has Been Identified: "Lich Stealer" (aka The Lich)  A stealer written in Python is being sold on an underground forum, claiming Chrome App Bound Encryption (V10/V11/V20+) bypass via LSASS identity impersonation.  Steals: browser creds, cookies, CC, 40+ wallet extensions, Discord tokens, FTP/VPN configs  Drops a clipper in System32 (XOR + Base64) hijacking 20+ crypto address types  Disables Defender, plus anti VM, anti debug, anti sandbox  Exfil via Telegram bot and Discord webhook (LOG.zip)  Persistence: HKCU...\Run + fake error decoy  #ThreatIntel #InfoStealer #Malware #Cybersecurity #ABE #Chrome #ThreatMon

🚨 Threat Actor Claims RCE and Data Leak of ICAI Exam Portal  A darkweb forum post reveals that an unknown threat actor has allegedly gained full remote code execution (RCE) and superadmin access to the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) exam portal, specifically examiners.icaiexam.icai.org. According to the post, the actor claims to have extracted over 200,000 records, including answer sheets from students who appeared for the May 2026 examinations.  The actor alleges the following capabilities and data exposure: - Full remote code execution and superadmin privileges on the exam portal - Access to answer sheets for over 200,000 students from the May 2026 exams - Ability to modify individual question marks, overall paper scores, examiner profiles, and official marking schemes  The post states that the breach was conducted "for fun" rather than for financial gain, and the actor explicitly notes they are not selling the data. A sample of the leaked answer sheets was reportedly shared via a Mega.nz folder link.  The ICAI is India's premier professional accounting body, regulating over 300,000 chartered accountants and conducting highly competitive national-level examinations. If authentic, the exposure of student answer sheets and the alleged capability to alter examination results could severely undermine the integrity of the certification process and compromise sensitive personal information.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of the ICAI exam portal has not been independently verified.  #DataBreach #ICAI #EducationSecurity #RCE #DarkWeb #IndiaCyber

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 4.3M Chinese Investor Records Leaked on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post claims that a database containing personal and financial information of 4.3 million Chinese investors has been exposed, allegedly sourced from China Securities, one of China's largest securities firms.  According to the listing on an underground forum, the database contains highly sensitive personally identifiable information and financial data for each investor. The post enumerates the following data fields:  - Investor Name - Investor Gender - Investor Mobile Phone - Investor ID Card Number - ID Card Address - Stock Holdings I, II, and III - Total Assets (RMB) - Floating Profit/Loss (RMB) - Investment Type  The post includes sample records showing Chinese names, phone numbers, national ID card numbers, residential addresses, specific stock holdings with ticker symbols, and account balances in Chinese yuan. The data reportedly spans multiple provinces including Beijing, Shanghai, Henan, Liaoning, and Shandong.  China Securities Co., Ltd. is one of the largest and most prominent securities firms in China, with significant market share in brokerage, investment banking, and asset management services. If authentic, the exposure of millions of investor records could enable identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns against Chinese retail investors.  The forum post directs interested parties to contact the seller via Telegram for pricing and additional data, though no specific ransom or sale price is listed in the thread.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and China Securities has not issued any public statement regarding the incident.  #DataLeak #ChinaSecurities #FinanceBreach #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity #DataBreach #ChinaCyber

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Access to Kraken User Data on Darkweb Forum  A darkweb forum post claims to offer access to a database containing personal information of Kraken cryptocurrency exchange users from multiple countries, including the United States and United Kingdom.  According to the listing on an underground forum, the seller alleges possession of "all private and fresh" data from Kraken, with samples provided for users in the US and UK. The post advertises availability of records from other countries upon request.  The sample data displayed in the thread includes the following fields for each record:  - User ID - First name - Last name - Country - Email address - Phone number - Trading pair information - Exchange identifier (KRAKEN)  The UK sample records show trading pairs including LTC_EUR, BTC_EUR, ETC_USD, GNO_EUR, BCH_EUR, ETH_CAD, DASH_USD, BTC_USD, ZEC_EUR, LTC_BTC, XRP_USD, XLM_EUR, XRP_EUR, BCH_USD, XRP_BTC, and BTC_GBP. The US sample records display various trading pairs such as ALIS_DOGE, BAM_DOGE, MTLMC3_USD, BTG_BTC, MCO_ETH, SCL_DOGE, NMR_DOGE, CXT_DOGE, ICX_BTC, and STORJ_ETH, among others.  Kraken is one of the world's largest cryptocurrency exchanges by trading volume, founded in 2011 and headquartered in San Francisco, California. The platform serves millions of users globally and is known for its security-focused approach and regulatory compliance. If authentic, exposure of user databases containing PII and trading activity could enable targeted phishing campaigns, account takeover attempts, and social engineering attacks against cryptocurrency traders.  The forum post directs interested parties to a Telegram channel for test samples, though no verification of the data's authenticity or origin has been provided. The seller does not specify how the data was obtained or the total number of records available.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of Kraken user data has not been independently verified, and Kraken has not issued any public statement regarding this claim.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 107M+ Records from 20 Iranian Travel Agencies for Sale  A darkweb forum post claims the sale of over 107 million personal records belonging to customers of 20 major Iranian travel agencies. The listing alleges the dataset is available for $60,000 in Bitcoin and reportedly includes a sample archive hosted on a third-party cloud storage service.  According to the post, the compromised organizations and their alleged record counts include: - Haftorang: 47.7M records - Tikban: 23.6M records - Rahbal Aseman: 4.1M records - Karmania: 3.9M records - SnappTrip: 3.6M records - Avan Gasht: 2.9M records - Behparvaz: 2.4M records - Sepehr Jam: 1.7M records - Holiday: 1.3M records - Peyk Khorshid: 930K records - Safariran: 720K records - Alaedin Travel: 550K records - Pharos Tour: 525K records - Nivar Parvaz: 309K records - Aseman Sepid: 200K records - Sainaseir: 116K records - Karvan Havaei: 105K records - Aseman Parvaz: 42K records - Ali Falak: 25K records  The actor alleges the data contains comprehensive customer profiles and detailed booking histories. According to the listing, the exposed information reportedly includes: - User profiles with names, dates of birth, email addresses, mobile and landline numbers, national ID numbers, passport numbers, and places of birth - Complete flight ticket details including passenger itineraries, flight numbers, seat assignments, and pricing - Train and bus ticket records with carriage types, driver information, and travel routes - Hotel and accommodation reservations featuring check-in/check-out times, guest counts, and property details  Several of the named entities, such as Haftorang, Tikban, and SnappTrip, operate as prominent travel service providers in Iran. If authentic, the exposure of such extensive travel and identification data could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and physical tracking of individuals across domestic and international routes.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach and data sale have not been independently verified. The forum post does not provide technical details regarding the initial access method or the timeframe of the alleged compromise.  #DataSale #IranCyber #TravelSecurity #PII #DarkWeb #SnappTrip #ThreatIntelligence

sample_tweets.text: ⚠️ Alleged Windows Kernel Exploit Framework Leaked by Orcinus Orca  A threat actor linked to Orcinus Orca has published what they claim is the full source code of "OrcaHunter Kernel Framework v1.0" alongside a purported pre-auth remote RCE/DoS proof-of-concept targeting Windows 11 24H2.  According to the post, the leak allegedly includes:  * Full OrcaHunter Framework v1.0 source code * 1,300+ lines of claimed exploit framework code * A pre-auth remote packet exploit targeting tcpip.sys * Windows 11 24H2 build 26100.8655 and below listed as targets * Core driver-related components * Automation modules for reverse engineering and kernel vulnerability research  The actor claims the framework is designed to support low-level kernel research, dynamic emulation, constraint solving, and automated analysis of Windows .sys binaries.  If authentic, the claimed capability could be highly significant due to the alleged targeting of the Windows networking stack. Pre-authentication vulnerabilities in network-facing components can create serious enterprise risk if weaponized.  Potential impact if validated:  * Remote code execution * Denial-of-service conditions * Kernel-level compromise * Pre-authentication attack surface exposure * Rapid weaponization by ransomware and intrusion groups * Increased targeting of Windows 11 enterprise environments  At this stage, the claim remains unverified. The existence of source code does not confirm a working zero-day or reliable exploit chain. Technical validation is required to determine whether the release contains a real vulnerability, a crash-only PoC, incomplete research code, or fabricated material.  Analyst Note: Exploit claims involving tcpip.sys and pre-auth remote execution should be treated as high-priority intelligence leads, but not as confirmed exploitation until independently reproduced. The most important next step is controlled technical validation in an isolated lab environment.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Windows

⚠️ Cuties AI User Database Reposted on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor has reposted and redistributed a processed CSV version of the previously disclosed Cuties AI breach, making the dataset more accessible to threat actors and data brokers.  According to the forum post, the breach originally surfaced in March 2026 and allegedly affected the NSFW AI companion platform Cuties AI.  Alleged exposed data includes:  * 780,207 records * Email addresses * User display names * Avatars * AI image generation prompts * Generated content references and URLs * Subscription and account metadata * Profile preferences * Account identifiers and platform activity data  The threat actor claims the processed CSV version was created to simplify searching, filtering, and analysis of the leaked information.  Potential risks include:  * User identification and deanonymization * Credential stuffing attacks * Targeted phishing campaigns * Privacy violations * Extortion and harassment attempts * Exposure of sensitive personal interests and AI-generated content history  Analyst Note: Breaches involving AI companion and adult-content platforms often carry elevated privacy risks because user identities can potentially be linked to highly sensitive behavioral data, prompts, preferences, and generated content. Even when financial information is absent, reputational and extortion risks can be significant.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #DataBreach

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-19 08:37 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 고난도 제로데이 취약점뿐만 아니라, 인터넷에 노출된 관리자 페이지나 잘못 설정된 원격 접속 서비스 등 기본적인 보안 허점을 우선적으로 악용하고 있습니다. 특히 AI 에이전트의 인증 프로세스를 우회하거나, 신뢰받는 플랫폼(GitHub, npm, Steam Workshop)에 악성 패키지 및 평판 조작형 악성코드를 유포하는 공급망 공격이 급증하고 있습니다. 또한, EDR 탐지를 회피하기 위한 전용 킬러 도구를 사용하는 랜섬웨어와, AI 분석을 방해하기 위해 악성코드 내부에 금지된 텍스트를 삽입하는 등 공격 기법이 더욱 고도화되고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **UNC6508**: 인터넷에 노출된 REDCap 서버를 타겟으로 초기 침투 및 백도어 배포 활동 수행.
- **The Gentlemen**: EDR 킬러를 활용하여 방어 체계를 무력화하는 RaaS(Ransomware-as-a-Service) 그룹.
- **Evil Corp**: SocGholish 봇넷을 운영하며 대규모 악성코드 유포 캠페인 수행 (최근 서버 차단 조치됨).
- **Kimsuky**: 한국 기업을 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행.
- **FishMonger**: SprySOCKS 백도어 및 커널 드라이버를 활용한 고도화된 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **AhnLab ASEC**: 대한민국 방산 산업 기밀 자료 유출 시도 및 국내 빅데이터 솔루션 기업 대상 랜섬웨어 공격 확인.
- **이스트시큐리티(ESRC)**: 북한 연계 해킹 그룹(Kimsuky)의 스피어 피싱 공격 및 Chai.js 위장 npm 악성 패키지(chai-as-init) 유포 확인.
- **기타**: 개인정보 수집 동의서를 사칭한 악성 LNK 파일 유포 등 국내 사용자 대상 사회공학적 기법이 지속적으로 탐지됨.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-48907**: Joomla JCE, CVSS 10.0, 활발히 악용 중.
- **CVE-2026-20253**: Splunk Enterprise, CVSS 9.8, 원격 코드 실행(RCE) 악용 중.
- **CVE-2026-42530**: NGINX Open Source, Critical, 원격 코드 실행(RCE) 가능.
- **CVE-2026-50656 (RoguePlanet)**: Microsoft Defender, Critical, SYSTEM 권한 탈취 가능.
- **CVE-2026-0257**: PAN-OS GlobalProtect, Critical, 활발히 악용 중.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**: 
    - CVE-2026-42530 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/f5-patches-two-critical-nginx-open.html))
    - CVE-2026-50656 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207239))
- **도메인/해시**: 이번 수집 데이터 내 직접적인 도메인 및 파일 해시 값은 명시되지 않음. (공격 도구 및 페이로드 분석 보고서 참조 필요)

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **패치 적용**: Splunk Enterprise(CVE-2026-20253), Joomla(CVE-2026-48907), NGINX 등 최신 보안 패치를 즉시 적용하십시오.
- **노출 점검**: 인터넷에 노출된 관리자 페이지, DB, 원격 접속 서비스(SSH 등)의 접근 제어를 강화하고 불필요한 노출을 제거하십시오.
- **공급망 보안**: 개발 환경에서 사용하는 npm 패키지 및 오픈소스 라이브러리의 무결성을 검증하고, 자동화된 AI 에이전트의 권한을 최소화하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8.5/10
- **근거**: 다수의 Critical 등급 제로데이 취약점이 야생에서 활발히 악용되고 있으며, 공급망 공격과 랜섬웨어의 결합으로 인해 기업 보안 환경이 매우 취약한 상태임.
